`retry_limit`\n- `model`\n\nExecution model:\n- Python decides what runs next\n- Claude only fills content where needed\n- pure programmatic steps bypass Claude\n- resume logic should skip steps whose existing artifacts already pass gates\n\n## Gate requirements\n\nGate tiers described:\n- `EXEMPT`\n- `LIGHT`\n- `STANDARD`\n- `STRICT`\n\nGate mode:\n- `BLOCKING` for outputs consumed by downstream steps\n- `TRAILING` for quality-only checks\n\nGate design is expected to verify:\n- required frontmatter fields\n- minimum line counts\n- semantic checks\n- structure and section presence\n\nFrontmatter patterns are first-class and meant to be validated programmatically.\n\n## What counts as programmatic vs Claude-assisted\n\nThe protocol is explicit:\n\nPure programmatic candidates:\n- repo/file enumeration\n- file/domain classification\n- batch assignment\n- filtering prior-pass results\n- config construction\n- structural validation\n- convergence/arithmetic scoring\n\nClaude-assisted candidates:\n- content analysis\n- report writing\n- synthesis\n- debate\n- executive summaries\n- rubric-based judgments\n\nHybrid:\n- programmatic setup\n- Claude content generation\n- strict programmatic validation after\n\n## Code templates available\n\nConcrete templates exist for:\n- `__init__.py`\n- `models.py`\n- `gates.py`\n- `prompts.py`\n- `config.py`\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/<name>/`\n\nThe required 12 generated package files are:\n\n1. `models.py`\n2. `gates.py`\n3. `prompts.py`\n4. `config.py`\n5. `monitor.py`\n6. `process.py`\n7. `executor.py`\n8. `tui.py`\n9. `logging_.py`\n10. `diagnostics.py`\n11. `commands.py`\n12. `__init__.py`\n\nOptional, workflow-dependent extras:\n- `inventory.py` — only if the workflow has discovery steps\n- `filtering.py` — only if the workflow has inter-step filtering\n\nAdditional non-package outputs mentioned by the protocol:\n- `portify-analysis.md`\n- `portify-spec.md`\n- optional `portify-prompts.md`\n- structural test file\n- `portify-summary.md`\n- `main.py` integration patch\n- protocol artifacts in newer roadmap language such as `portify-analysis.yaml`, `portify-prerequisites.yaml`, `portify-integration.yaml`\n\n## Dependency order\n\nThe explicit generation order is:\n\n1. `models.py`\n2. `gates.py`\n3. `prompts.py`\n4. `config.py`\n5. `monitor.py`\n6. `process.py`\n7. `executor.py`\n8. `tui.py`\n9. `logging_.py`\n10. `diagnostics.py`\n11. `commands.py`\n12. `__init__.py`\n\nDependency rationale from the templates:\n- `models.py`: no internal deps\n- `gates.py`: imports models/shared pipeline types\n- `prompts.py`: imports config/model types\n- `config.py`: imports models\n- `monitor.py`: imports models\n- `process.py`: imports models and config; extends shared pipeline `ClaudeProcess`\n- `executor.py`: imports nearly everything above\n- `tui.py`: imports models\n- `logging_.py`: imports models\n- `diagnostics.py`: imports models\n- `commands.py`: imports config and executor\n- `__init__.py`: re-exports command group\n\n## What each file is supposed to contain\n\n### 1) `models.py`\nPurpose:\n- Domain model layer for the generated pipeline.\n\nExpected contents:\n- Domain-specific status enum, typically extending sprint-style states:\n  - `PENDING`\n  - `RUNNING`\n  - `PASS`\n  - `PASS_NO_SIGNAL`\n  - `PASS_NO_REPORT`\n  - `INCOMPLETE`\n  - `HALT`\n  - `TIMEOUT`\n  - `ERROR`\n  - `SKIPPED`\n- Aggregate outcome enum, typically:\n  - `SUCCESS`\n  - `HALTED`\n  - `INTERRUPTED`\n  - `ERROR`\n- Optional workflow/domain enums\n- Config dataclass extending `PipelineConfig`\n- Step subclass extending `Step`\n- Step result dataclass extending `StepResult`\n- Aggregate pipeline result model\n- Monitor state dataclass\n\nExpected key classes/functions/properties:\n- `{PREFIX}Status(Enum)`\n- `{PREFIX}Outcome(Enum)`\n- `{PREFIX}Config(PipelineConfig)`\n- `{PREFIX}Step(Step)`\n- `{PREFIX}StepResult(StepResult)`\n- `{PREFIX}Result`\n- `{PREFIX}MonitorState`\n- `resume_command()`\n- computed properties like:\n  - `duration_seconds`\n  - `steps_passed`\n  - `steps_failed`\n  - config path properties\n\nNotable requirements:\n- Config should hold workflow-specific fields such as source path, pass selection, batch sizes, stall policies.\n- Result model should support resume semantics and suggested resume budget.\n- Monitor state should hold NDJSON-derived runtime signals.\n\n### 2) `gates.py`\nPurpose:\n- Programmatic validation of step outputs.\n\nExpected contents:\n- Gate definitions for each output artifact\n- Semantic check functions\n- Registry of all gates\n\nExpected key elements:\n- `GateCriteria` objects per step/output\n- `SemanticCheck` entries\n- `ALL_GATES: dict[str, GateCriteria]`\n- semantic check functions like:\n  - heading validation\n  - section presence\n  - cross-reference checks\n  - structural field checks\n\nImportant requirement:\n- The protocol skill says semantic checks should return `tuple[bool, str]` because the reason string feeds diagnostics/retries.\n- However, parts of `pipeline-spec.md` and `code-templates.md` still show older `Callable[[str], bool]` wording.\n- So there is a spec inconsistency:\n  - authoritative behavioral text: `tuple[bool, str]`\n  - some examples/templates: `bool`\n- Since the protocol repeatedly emphasizes reason strings, the intended current requirement appears to be `tuple[bool, str]`.\n\n### 3) `prompts.py`\nPurpose:\n- Build prompt contracts for each Claude-assisted step.\n\nExpected contents:\n- One prompt builder function per Claude-assisted step\n- Optional depth instruction constants\n- Input embedding logic for small vs large files\n\nExpected key functions:\n- `build_<step>_prompt(...) -> str`\n- possible helper similar to `_embed_inputs(...)`\n\nPrompt requirements:\n- Specify embedded context or file args\n- Require explicit output structure\n- Require frontmatter fields\n- Require machine-readable markers:\n  - `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE`\n  - `EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT`\n- Encode structural expectations the gate will verify\n\nIf prompts are large:\n- split into a separate `portify-prompts.md` during spec phase\n\n### 4) `config.py`\nPurpose:\n- Resolve CLI arguments into a validated config object.\n\nExpected contents:\n- Config loader\n- path resolution\n- defaults\n- validation\n- possibly workflow-specific discovery helpers\n\nExpected key functions:\n- `load_<cli_name>_config(...) -> {PREFIX}Config`\n- helper functions for:\n  - input validation\n  - default path derivation\n  - file discovery\n  - CLI argument normalization\n\nRequirements mentioned in protocol:\n- CLI arg resolution\n- file discovery\n- config construction\n- validation logic\n- default output path behavior is clarified in:\n  - `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/.claude/skills/sc-cli-portify-protocol/decisions.yaml`\n\n### 5) `monitor.py`\nPurpose:\n- Live monitoring of Claude subprocess output.\n\nExpected contents:\n- Incremental NDJSON output parser\n- signal extraction\n- growth/stall tracking\n- max-turns error detection\n\nExpected key classes/functions:\n- `OutputMonitor`\n- `detect_error_max_turns(...)`\n- state-reset/start/stop/get_state methods\n\nRequirements:\n- Follow sprint monitor pattern closely\n- Adapt only signal extraction to the target domain\n- Feed runtime state into TUI and executor stall logic\n\n### 6) `process.py`\nPurpose:\n- Domain-specific Claude subprocess wrapper.\n\nExpected contents:\n- subclass of shared pipeline `ClaudeProcess`\n- domain-specific prompt assembly\n- signal handling or process wrapper utilities\n\nExpected key classes/functions:\n- `{PREFIX}Process(ClaudeProcess)`\n- `build_prompt()`\n- `SignalHandler`\n\nRequirements:\n- use sprint process pattern\n- construct prompt with proper task framing\n- support subprocess isolation patterns\n- likely integrate context injection from earlier step results\n\n### 7) `executor.py`\nPurpose:\n- The orchestration brain of the generated pipeline.\n\nExpected contents:\n- synchronous supervisor loop\n- step graph builder\n- launch/monitor/wait/classify cycle\n- gate evaluation\n- diagnostics handoff\n- halt/resume logic\n- optional routing for pure-programmatic steps\n\nExpected key functions:\n- `execute_<cli_name>(config) -> {PREFIX}Result`\n- `_build_steps(config)`\n- `_determine_status(exit_code, step, config)`\n- possibly `execute_step(...)`\n- possibly `run_programmatic_step(...)`\n- batch parallel execution using `ThreadPoolExecutor`\n\nCritical requirements:\n- synchronous only; no `asyncio`\n- `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallelism\n- `time.sleep()` polling loops\n- per-step monitoring and stall detection\n- turn-budget guards with `TurnLedger`\n- runner-authored truth\n- subprocess isolation\n- context injection\n- resume-first failure behavior\n\n### 8) `tui.py`\nPurpose:\n- Rich live dashboard during pipeline execution.\n\nExpected contents:\n- live display\n- step table\n- progress bar\n- active step details\n- gate display states/styles\n\nExpected key classes/elements:\n- `{PREFIX}TUI`\n- gate display state machine, likely with states like:\n  - `NONE`\n  - `CHECKING`\n  - `PASS`\n  - `FAIL_DEFERRED`\n  - `REMEDIATING`\n  - `REMEDIATED`\n  - `HALT`\n\nRequirements:\n- follow sprint TUI pattern\n- show monitoring state, progress, current step, gate state\n\n### 9) `logging_.py`\nPurpose:\n- Durable execution logs.\n\nExpected contents:\n- JSONL log writer\n- Markdown log writer\n- screen/log sync\n\nExpected key classes/functions:\n- `{PREFIX}Logger`\n- methods like:\n  - `write_header()`\n  - `write_step_start(step)`\n  - `write_step_result(result)`\n  - `write_summary(result)`\n\nRequirements:\n- dual JSONL + Markdown logging\n- align with sprint logging pattern\n\n### 10) `diagnostics.py`\nPurpose:\n- Failure analysis after step or gate problems.\n\nExpected contents:\n- failure bundle collection\n- classification\n- report generation\n\nExpected key classes:\n- `DiagnosticCollector`\n- `FailureClassifier`\n- `ReportGenerator`\n\nRequirements:\n- runner-side diagnostics only\n- should not consume Claude turn budget\n- adapt failure categories to the target pipeline domain\n\n### 11) `commands.py`\nPurpose:\n- Click entrypoints for the generated CLI subcommand.\n\nExpected contents:\n- Click group\n- `run` command\n- options and arguments\n- dry-run renderer\n\nExpected key functions:\n- `@click.group(\"{CLI_NAME}\")`\n- `{GROUP_NAME}_group()`\n- `run(...)`\n- `_print_dry_run(config)`\n\nRequirements:\n- imports config loader and executor\n- exits with success/failure based on aggregate result\n- should expose workflow-specific CLI options\n\n### 12) `__init__.py`\nPurpose:\n- Package export surface.\n\nExpected contents:\n- import of the Click group\n- `__all__`\n\nExpected key export:\n- `{GROUP_NAME}_group`\n\n## Shared base classes and pipeline primitives the generated files rely on\n\nThe generated module is expected to reuse shared infrastructure from `superclaude.cli.pipeline`, especially:\n- `PipelineConfig`\n- `Step`\n- `StepResult`\n- `GateMode`\n- `GateCriteria`\n- `SemanticCheck`\n- `gate_passed()`\n- `ClaudeProcess`\n\nAnd for turn budgeting, the protocol explicitly clarifies:\n- `TurnLedger` is not in `superclaude.cli.pipeline.models`\n- it must be imported from:\n  - `superclaude.cli.sprint.models`\n\nThat is stated in:\n- `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/.claude/skills/sc-cli-portify-protocol/decisions.yaml`\n\n## Step graph and execution requirements\n\nThe portify spec expects workflow operations to be expressed as:\n- single `Step` objects for sequential work\n- `list[Step]` groups for parallel work\n- dynamic `build_steps()` when step count is only known at runtime\n\nExpected `Step` fields from the reference pattern:\n- `id`\n- `prompt`\n- `output_file`\n- `gate`\n- `gate_mode`\n- `timeout_seconds`\n- `inputs`\n- `retry_limit`\n- `model`\n\nExecution model:\n- Python decides what runs next\n- Claude only fills content where needed\n- pure programmatic steps bypass Claude\n- resume logic should skip steps whose existing artifacts already pass gates\n\n## Gate requirements\n\nGate tiers described:\n- `EXEMPT`\n- `LIGHT`\n- `STANDARD`\n- `STRICT`\n\nGate mode:\n- `BLOCKING` for outputs consumed by downstream steps\n- `TRAILING` for quality-only checks\n\nGate design is expected to verify:\n- required frontmatter fields\n- minimum line counts\n- semantic checks\n- structure and section presence\n\nFrontmatter patterns are first-class and meant to be validated programmatically.\n\n## What counts as programmatic vs Claude-assisted\n\nThe protocol is explicit:\n\nPure programmatic candidates:\n- repo/file enumeration\n- file/domain classification\n- batch assignment\n- filtering prior-pass results\n- config construction\n- structural validation\n- convergence/arithmetic scoring\n\nClaude-assisted candidates:\n- content analysis\n- report writing\n- synthesis\n- debate\n- executive summaries\n- rubric-based judgments\n\nHybrid:\n- programmatic setup\n- Claude content generation\n- strict programmatic validation after\n\n## Code templates available\n\nConcrete templates exist for:\n- `__init__.py`\n- `models.py`\n- `gates.py`\n- `prompts.py`\n- `config.py`\n-  178→        return None\n   179→\n   180→\n   181→# --- Monitor State ---\n   182→\n   183→@dataclass\n   184→class {PREFIX}MonitorState:\n   185→    \"\"\"Live execution state fed by the output monitor.\"\"\"\n   186→    output_bytes: int = 0\n   187→    output_bytes_prev: int = 0\n   188→    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n   189→    last_event_time: float = 0.0\n   190→    step_started_at: 196→\n   197→    @property\n   198→    def stall_status(self) -> str:\n   199→        if self.events_received == 0:\n   200→            elapsed = time.time() - self.step_started_at if self.step_started_at else 0\n   201→            return \"waiting...\" if elapsed < 30 else \"STALLED\"\n   202→        if self.stall_seconds > 120:\n   203→            return \"STALLED\"\n   204→        if self.stall_seconds > 30:\n   205→            return \"thinking...\"\n   206→        return \"active\"\n   207→\n   208→    @property\n   209→    def output_size_display(self) -> str:\n   210→        if self.output_bytes < 1024:\n   211→            return f\"{self.output_bytes}B\"\n   212→        return f\"{self.output_bytes / 1024:.1f}KB\"\n   213→```\n   214→\n   215→## Template: `gates.py`\n watchdog\n   316→- Signal-aware graceful shutdown\n   317→- Deterministic status classification\n   318→- Failure diagnostics\n   319→\n   320→IMPORTANT: Use synchronous execution with threading, NOT async/await.\n   321→Sprint uses time.sleep() polling loops and concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor\n   322→for parallel batch dispatch. Do not use asyncio.\n   323→\n   324→Source workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n   325→\"\"\"\n   326→\n   327→from __future__ import annotations\n   328→\n   329→import shutil\n   330→import signal\n   331→import sys\n   332→import time\n   333→from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\n   334→from pathlib import Path\n   335→\n   336→from superclaude.cli.pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n   337→\n   338→from .config import load_{CLI_NAME}_config\n   339→from .diagnostics import DiagnosticCollector, FailureClassifier, ReportGenerator\n   340→from .logging_ import {PREFIX}Logger\n   341→from .models import (\n   342→    {PREFIX}Config,\n   343→    {PREFIX}MonitorState,\n   344→    {PREFIX}Outcome,\n   345→    {PREFIX}Result,\n   346→    {PREFIX}Status,\n   347→    {PREFIX}StepResult,\n   348→)\n   349→from .monitor import OutputMonitor, detect_error_max_turns\n   350→from .process import {PREFIX}Process, SignalHandler\n   351→from .tui import {PREFIX}TUI\n   352→\n   353→\n   354→def execute_{CLI_NAME}(config: {PREFIX}Config) -> {PREFIX}Result:\n   355→    \"\"\"Execute the {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline with supervised monitoring.\"\"\"\n   356→\n   357→    # Pre-flight\n   358→    if not shutil.which(\"claude\"):\n   359→        print(\"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH\", file=sys.stderr)\n   360→        sys.exit(1)\n   361→\n   362→    # Setup infrastructure\n   363→    handler = SignalHandler()\n   364→    logger = {PREFIX}Logger(config)\n   365→    tui = {PREFIX}TUI(config)\n   366→    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n   367→    result = {PREFIX}Result(config=config)\n   368→\n   369→    logger.write_header()\n   370→    tui.start()\n   371→\n   372→    try:\n   373→        for step in _build_steps(config):\n   374→            if handler.shutdown_requested:\n   375→                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.INTERRUPTED\n   376→                break\n   377→\n   378→            # Reset monitor for this step's output\n   379→            output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n   380→            monitor.reset(output_path)\n   381→            monitor.start()\n   382→\n   383→            # Launch subprocess\n   384→            process = {PREFIX}Process(config, step)\n   385→            logger.write_step_start(step)\n   386→            deadline = time.monotonic() + step.timeout_seconds\n   387→            process.start()\n   388→\n   389→            # Supervision loop\n   390→            while process.is_running():\n   391→                if handler.shutdown_requested:\n   392→                    process.stop()\n   393→                    break\n   394→\n   395→                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n   396→                    process.stop()\n   397→                    break\n   398→\n   399→                state = monitor.get_state()\n   400→\n   401→                # Stall watchdog\n   402→                if (config.stall_timeout\n   403→                        and state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n   404→                        and state.events_received > 0):\n   405→                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n   406→                        process.stop()\n   407→                        break\n   408→\n   409→                try:\n   410→                    tui.update(step, state)\n   411→                except Exception:\n   412→                    pass\n   413→\n   414→                time.sleep(0.5)\n   415→\n   416→            # Classify result\n   417→            exit_code = process.wait()\n   418→            monitor.stop()\n   419→            status = _determine_status(exit_code, step, config)\n   420→\n   421→            step_result = {PREFIX}StepResult(\n   422→                step=step,\n   423→                status=status,\n   424→                exit_code=exit_code,\n   425→                started_at=process.started_at,\n   426→                finished_at=time.time(),\n   427→                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n   428→            )\n   429→            result.step_results.append(step_result)\n   430→            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n   431→\n   432→            # Gate check for passing steps\n   433→            if status == {PREFIX}Status.PASS and step.gate:\n   434→                output_content = (config.work_dir / step.output_file).read_text()\n   435→                passed, reason = gate_passed(output_content, step.gate)\n   436→                if not passed:\n   437→                    step_result.status = {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n   438→                    step_result.gate_failure_reason = reason\n   439→                    status = {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n   440→\n   441→            # Handle failures\n   442→            if status not in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT):\n   443→                collector = DiagnosticCollector()\n   444→                bundle = collector.collect(step_result, monitor.state, config)\n   445→                classifier = FailureClassifier()\n   446→                category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n   447→                reporter = ReportGenerator()\n   448→                reporter.write_report(bundle, category, config)\n   449→\n   450→                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.HALTED\n   451→                result.halt_step = step.id\n   452→                break\n   453→\n   454→        # Finalize\n   455→        if result.outcome == {PREFIX}Outcome.SUCCESS:\n   456→            all_passed = all(\n   457→                r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT, {PREFIX}Status.SKIPPED)\n   458→                for r in result.step_results\n   459→            )\n   460→            if not all_passed:\n   461→                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.ERROR\n   462→\n   463→        result.finished_at = time.time()\n   464→        logger.write_summary(result)\n   465→\n   466→    finally:\n   467→        tui.stop()\n   468→        monitor.stop()\n   469→        handler.restore()\n   470→\n   471→    return result\n   472→\n   473→\n   474→{BUILD_STEPS_FUNCTION}\n   475→\n   476→\n   477→def _determine_status(\n   478→    exit_code: int | None,\n   479→    step,\n   480→    config: {PREFIX}Config,\n   481→) -> {PREFIX}Status:\n   482→    \"\"\"Classify step outcome from exit code and output artifacts.\"\"\"\n   483→    if exit_code == 124:\n   484→        return {PREFIX}Status.TIMEOUT\n   485→    if exit_code and exit_code != 0:\n   486→        return {PREFIX}Status.ERROR\n   487→\n   488→    result_path = config.work_dir / f\"{step.id}-result.md\"\n   489→    output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n   490→\n   491→    if result_path.exists():\n   492→        content = result_path.read_text()\n   493→        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n   494→            return {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n   495→        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n   496→            return {PREFIX}Status.PASS\n   497→        if \"status: PASS\" in content.lower():\n   498→            return {PREFIX}Status.PASS\n   499→        if \"status: FAIL\" in content.lower():\n   500→            return {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n   501→        return {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n   502→\n   503→    if output_path.exists():\n   504→        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n   505→            return {PREFIX}Status.INCOMPLETE\n   506→        return {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT\n   507→\n   508→    return {PREFIX}Status.ERROR\n   509→```\n   510→\n   511→## Template: `monitor.py`\n   512→\n   513→Follow the sprint monitor pattern exactly — incremental NDJSON tail with signal 585→\n   586→    console.print(table)\n   587→```\n   588→\n   589→## Integration Template: `main.py` Patch\n   590→\n   591→Add after the last existing import:\n   592→\n   593→```python\n   594→from Code Templates Reference\n\nLoaded during Phase 3 of portification. Contains the actual file templates used to generate each module in the CLI pipeline package.\n\nThese templates are parameterized. Placeholders use `{PARAM}` syntax. The skill fills these based on the pipeline specification from Phase 2.\n\n## File Generation Order\n\nGenerate in this order to respect dependencies:\n\n1. `models.py` — No internal deps\n2. `gates.py` — Imports from models\n3. `prompts.py` — Imports from models\n4. `config.py` — Imports from models\n5. `monitor.py` — Imports from models\n6. `process.py` — Imports from models, config; extends pipeline.process\n7. `executor.py` — Imports from everything above\n8. `tui.py` — Imports from models\n9. `logging_.py` — Imports from models\n10. `diagnostics.py` — Imports from models\n11. `commands.py` — Imports from config, executor\n12. `__init__.py` — Re-exports\n\n## Template: `__init__.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"CLI subcommand package for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nPortified from: StepStatus,\n)\n\n\n# --- Enums ---\n\nclass {PREFIX}Status(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Step-level status for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\n    Extends generic StepStatus with domain-specific states.\n    \"\"\"\n    PENDING = \"pending\"\n    RUNNING = \"running\"\n    PASS = \"pass\"\n    PASS_NO_SIGNAL = \"pass_no_signal\"\n    PASS_NO_REPORT = \"pass_no_report\"\n    INCOMPLETE = \"incomplete\"\n    HALT = \"halt\"\n    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"\n    ERROR = \"error\"\n    SKIPPED = \"skipped\"\n\n\nclass {PREFIX}Outcome(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate pipeline outcome.\"\"\"\n    SUCCESS = \"success\"\n    HALTED = \"halted\"\n    INTERRUPTED = \"interrupted\"\n    ERROR = \"error\"\n\n\n{DOMAIN_ENUMS}\n\n\n# --- Configuration ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}Config(PipelineConfig):\n    \"\"\"{PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline configuration.\n\n    Extends PipelineConfig with {PIPELINE_NAME}-specific settings.\n    \"\"\"\n    {CONFIG_FIELDS}\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        \"\"\"Ensure work_dir is set from pipeline-specific paths.\"\"\"\n        if not self.work_dir:\n            self.work_dir = self.{PRIMARY_PATH_FIELD}\n        self.work_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    {CONFIG_PROPERTIES}\n\n\n# --- Step Specialization ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}Step(Step):\n    \"\"\"{PIPELINE_NAME}-specific step with domain metadata.\"\"\"\n    {STEP_FIELDS}\n\n\n# --- Result Types ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}StepResult(StepResult):\n    \"\"\"Result for a single {PIPELINE_NAME} step.\n\n    Extends StepResult with execution output_bytes: int = 0\n    error_bytes: int = 0\n    {RESULT_FIELDS}\n\n    @property\n    def duration_seconds(self) -> float:\n        if self.started_at and self.finished_at:\n            return self.finished_at - self.started_at\n        return 0.0\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}Result:\n    \"\"\"Aggregate result for the entire {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\"\"\"\n    config: {PREFIX}Config\n    step_results: list[{PREFIX}StepResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    outcome: {PREFIX}Outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.SUCCESS\n    started_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)\n    finished_at: float | None = None\n    halt_step: str | None = None\n\n    @property\n    def duration_seconds(self) -> float:\n        end = self.finished_at or time.time()\n        return end - self.started_at\n\n    @property\n    def steps_passed(self) -> int:\n        return sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n                   if r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL))\n\n    @property\n    def steps_failed(self) -> int:\n        return sum(1 for r in self.step_results\n                   if r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.HALT, {PREFIX}Status.ERROR, {PREFIX}Status.TIMEOUT))\n\n    def resume_command(self) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Generate CLI command to resume from the failed step.\"\"\"\n        if self.halt_step:\n            return f\"superclaude {CLI_NAME} run --resume --start {self.halt_step}\"\n        return None\n\n\n# --- Monitor State ---\n\n@dataclass\nclass {PREFIX}MonitorState:\n    \"\"\"Live execution state fed by the output monitor.\"\"\"\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    output_bytes_prev: int = 0\n    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n    last_event_time: float = 0.0\n    step_started_at: float = 0.0\n    events_received: int = 0\n    lines_total: int = 0\n    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n    {MONITOR_FIELDS}\n\n    @property\n    def stall_status(self) -> str:\n        if self.events_received == 0:\n            elapsed = time.time() - self.step_started_at if self.step_started_at else 0\n            return \"waiting...\" if elapsed < 30 else \"STALLED\"\n        if self.stall_seconds > 120:\n            return \"STALLED\"\n        if self.stall_seconds > 30:\n            return \"thinking...\"\n        return \"active\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_size_display(self) -> str:\n        if self.output_bytes < 1024:\n            return f\"{self.output_bytes}B\"\n        return f\"{self.output_bytes / 1024:.1f}KB\"\n```\n\n## Template: annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import {PREFIX}Config\n\n{CONFIG_CONSTANTS}\n\n\ndef load_{CLI_NAME}_config(\n    {CONFIG_PARAMS}\n) -> {PREFIX}Config:\n    \"\"\"Construct and validate pipeline configuration from CLI arguments.\"\"\"\n    {CONFIG_BODY}\n    return config\n\n\n{CONFIG_HELPERS}\n```\n\n## Template: `executor.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint-style supervised executor for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nControls the execution loop with:\n- Per-step live monitoring\n- TUI refresh at ~2Hz\n- Stall detection and watchdog\n- Signal-aware graceful shutdown\n- Deterministic status classification\n- Failure diagnostics\n\nIMPORTANT: Use synchronous execution with threading, NOT async/await.\nSprint uses time.sleep() polling loops and concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor\nfor parallel batch dispatch. Do not use asyncio.\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport shutil\nimport signal\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n\nfrom .config import load_{CLI_NAME}_config\nfrom .diagnostics import DiagnosticCollector, FailureClassifier, ReportGenerator\nfrom .logging_ import {PREFIX}Logger\nfrom .models import (\n    {PREFIX}Config,\n    {PREFIX}MonitorState,\n    {PREFIX}Outcome,\n    {PREFIX}Result,\n    {PREFIX}Status,\n    {PREFIX}StepResult,\n)\nfrom .monitor import OutputMonitor, detect_error_max_turns\nfrom .process import {PREFIX}Process, SignalHandler\nfrom .tui import {PREFIX}TUI\n\n\ndef execute_{CLI_NAME}(config: {PREFIX}Config) -> {PREFIX}Result:\n    \"\"\"Execute the {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline with supervised monitoring.\"\"\"\n\n    # Pre-flight\n    if not shutil.which(\"claude\"):\n        print(\"Error: 'claude' binary not found in PATH\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Setup infrastructure\n    handler = SignalHandler()\n    logger = {PREFIX}Logger(config)\n    tui = {PREFIX}TUI(config)\n    monitor = OutputMonitor()\n    result = {PREFIX}Result(config=config)\n\n    logger.write_header()\n    tui.start()\n\n    try:\n        for step in _build_steps(config):\n            if handler.shutdown_requested:\n                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.INTERRUPTED\n                break\n\n            # Reset monitor for this step's output\n            output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n            monitor.reset(output_path)\n            monitor.start()\n\n            # Launch subprocess\n            process = {PREFIX}Process(config, step)\n            logger.write_step_start(step)\n            deadline = time.monotonic() + step.timeout_seconds\n            process.start()\n\n            # Supervision loop\n            while process.is_running():\n                if handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                state = monitor.get_state()\n\n                # Stall watchdog\n                if (config.stall_timeout\n                        and state.stall_seconds > config.stall_timeout\n                        and state.events_received > 0):\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        process.stop()\n                        break\n\n                try:\n                    tui.update(step, state)\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Classify result\n            exit_code = process.wait()\n            monitor.stop()\n            status = _determine_status(exit_code, step, config)\n\n            step_result = {PREFIX}StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=status,\n                exit_code=exit_code,\n                started_at=process.started_at,\n                finished_at=time.time(),\n                output_bytes=monitor.state.output_bytes,\n            )\n            result.step_results.append(step_result)\n            logger.write_step_result(step_result)\n\n            # Gate check for passing steps\n            if status == {PREFIX}Status.PASS and step.gate:\n                output_content = (config.work_dir / step.output_file).read_text()\n                passed, reason = gate_passed(output_content, step.gate)\n                if not passed:\n                    step_result.status = {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n                    step_result.gate_failure_reason = reason\n                    status = {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n\n            # Handle failures\n            if status not in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT):\n                collector = DiagnosticCollector()\n                bundle = collector.collect(step_result, monitor.state, config)\n                classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                reporter.write_report(bundle, category, config)\n\n                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.HALTED\n                result.halt_step = step.id\n                break\n\n        # Finalize\n        if result.outcome == {PREFIX}Outcome.SUCCESS:\n            all_passed = all(\n                r.status in ({PREFIX}Status.PASS, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL, {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT, {PREFIX}Status.SKIPPED)\n                for r in result.step_results\n            )\n            if not all_passed:\n                result.outcome = {PREFIX}Outcome.ERROR\n\n        result.finished_at = time.time()\n        logger.write_summary(result)\n\n    finally:\n        tui.stop()\n        monitor.stop()\n        handler.restore()\n\n    return result\n\n\n{BUILD_STEPS_FUNCTION}\n\n\ndef _determine_status(\n    exit_code: int | None,\n    step,\n    config: {PREFIX}Config,\n) -> {PREFIX}Status:\n    \"\"\"Classify step outcome from exit code and output artifacts.\"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return {PREFIX}Status.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code and exit_code != 0:\n        return {PREFIX}Status.ERROR\n\n    result_path = config.work_dir / f\"{step.id}-result.md\"\n    output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n\n    if result_path.exists():\n        content = result_path.read_text()\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n            return {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n            return {PREFIX}Status.PASS\n        if \"status: PASS\" in content.lower():\n            return {PREFIX}Status.PASS\n        if \"status: FAIL\" in content.lower():\n            return {PREFIX}Status.HALT\n        return {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_path.exists():\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n            return {PREFIX}Status.INCOMPLETE\n        return {PREFIX}Status.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return {PREFIX}Status.ERROR\n```\n\n## Template: `monitor.py`\n\nFollow the sprint monitor pattern exactly — incremental NDJSON tail with signal extraction. See `src/superclaude/cli/sprint/monitor.py` for the implementation. Adapt signal patterns for the domain.\n\n## Template: `process.py`\n\nFollow the sprint process pattern — subclass pipeline `ClaudeProcess`, specialize the constructor for domain config, and implement `build_prompt()` that constructs the step's prompt with proper `/sc:task-unified` framing. See `src/superclaude/cli/sprint/process.py`.\n\n## Template: `tui.py`\n\nFollow the sprint TUI pattern — Rich Live display with header, step table, progress bar, and active step panel. Adapt the status styles and display fields for the domain. See `src/superclaude/cli/sprint/tui.py`.\n\n## Template: `logging_.py`\n\nFollow the sprint logging pattern — dual JSONL + Markdown logging with screen output. See `src/superclaude/cli/sprint/logging_.py`.\n\n## Template: `diagnostics.py`\n\nFollow the sprint diagnostics pattern — `DiagnosticCollector`, `FailureClassifier`, `ReportGenerator`. Adapt failure categories for the domain. See `src/superclaude/cli/sprint/diagnostics.py`.\n\n## Template: `commands.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"Click CLI commands for {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\n\nProvides the `superclaude {CLI_NAME}` command group.\n\nSource workflow: {SOURCE_SKILL}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport sys\n\nimport click\n\nfrom .config import load_{CLI_NAME}_config\nfrom .executor import execute_{CLI_NAME}\n\n\n@click.group(\"{CLI_NAME}\")\ndef {GROUP_NAME}_group():\n    \"\"\"{PIPELINE_DESCRIPTION}\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\n@{GROUP_NAME}_group.command(\"run\")\n{CLICK_ARGUMENTS}\n{CLICK_OPTIONS}\ndef run({RUN_PARAMS}):\n    \"\"\"Execute the {PIPELINE_NAME} pipeline.\"\"\"\n    config = load_{CLI_NAME}_config({CONFIG_ARGS})\n\n    if dry_run:\n        _print_dry_run(config)\n        return\n\n    result = execute_{CLI_NAME}(config)\n    sys.exit(0 if result.outcome.value == \"success\" else 1)\n\n\ndef _print_dry_run(config):\n    \"\"\"Display execution plan without running.\"\"\"\n    from rich.console import Console\n    from rich.table import Table\n\n    console = Console()\n    table = Table(title=f\"{PIPELINE_NAME} Pipeline Plan\")\n    table.add_column(\"Step\", style=\"cyan\")\n    table.add_column(\"Output\", style=\"green\")\n    table.add_column(\"Gate\", style=\"yellow\")\n    table.add_column(\"Timeout\", style=\"dim\")\n\n    {DRY_RUN_ROWS}\n\n    console.print(table)\n```\n\n## Integration Template: `main.py` Patch\n\nAdd after the last existing import:\n\n```python\nfrom superclaude.cli.{MODULE_NAME} import {GROUP_NAME}_group\n```\n\nAdd after the last existing `app.add_command()`:\n\n```python\napp.add_command({GROUP_NAME}_group)\n```\n","numLines":602,"startLine":1,"totalLines":602}}}

   Produces structured markdown containing:\n   207→    - Prior task results (status, gate outcome, key details)\n   208→    - Gate outcomes (pass/fail/deferred per prior task)\n   209→    - 213→    prior results exceeds ``compress_threshold`` (default 3): older tasks\n   214→    are reduced to a one-line summary while recent tasks retain full detail.\n   215→\n   216→    Args:\n   217→ 277→            timeout=10,\n   278→        )\n   279→        if result.returncode != 0 or not result.stdout.strip():\n   280→            return \"\"\n   281→        return (\n   282→            \"### {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_iuu6A06HtS1CwEgNhEuaxwtF","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n     2→\n     3→Validates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n     4→  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n     5→  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n     6→  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n     7→  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n     8→\n     9→NFR-003: No subprocess import. NFR-007: No sprint/roadmap imports.\n    10→\"\"\"\n    11→\n    12→from __future__ import annotations\n    13→\n    14→import re\n    15→from pathlib import Path\n    16→\n    17→from .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n    18→\n    19→\n    20→def gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    21→    \"\"\"Validate a step's output against its gate criteria.\n    22→\n    23→    Returns (True, None) on pass.\n    24→    Returns (False, reason) on failure where reason is human-readable.\n    25→    \"\"\"\n    26→    tier = criteria.enforcement_tier\n    27→\n    28→    # EXEMPT: always passes\n    29→    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n    30→        return True, None\n    31→\n    32→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    33→    if not output_file.exists():\n    34→        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n    35→\n    36→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    37→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    38→    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n    39→        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n    40→\n    41→    # LIGHT stops here\n    42→    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n    43→        return True, None\n    44→\n    45→    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    46→    lines = content.splitlines()\n    47→    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n    48→        return False, (\n    49→            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n    50→            f\"in {output_file}\"\n    51→        )\n    52→\n    53→    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    54→    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n    55→        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file)\n    56→        if not ok:\n    57→            return False, reason\n    58→\n    59→    # STANDARD stops here\n    60→    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n    61→        return True, None\n    62→\n    63→    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    64→    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n    65→        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n    66→            if not check.check_fn(content):\n    67→                return False, f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\"\n    68→\n    69→    return True, None\n    70→\n    71→\n    72→_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(\n    73→    r\"^---[ \\t]*\\n((?:[ \\t]*\\w[\\w\\s]*:.*\\n)+)---[ \\t]*$\",\n    74→    re.MULTILINE,\n    75→)\n    76→\n    77→\n    78→def _check_frontmatter(\n    79→    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n    80→) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    81→    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\n    82→\n    83→    Uses regex with re.MULTILINE to discover frontmatter anywhere in the\n    84→    document (not just byte 0), allowing conversational preamble before the\n    85→    ``---`` delimiters.  Horizontal rules (``---`` with no ``key: value``\n    86→    content) are rejected because the pattern requires at least one\n    87→    ``key: value`` line between delimiters.\n    88→    \"\"\"\n    89→    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n    90→    if match is None:\n    91→        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter not found in {output_file}\"\n    92→\n    93→    frontmatter_text = match.group(1)\n    94→\n    95→    # Extract keys from key: value lines\n    96→    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    97→    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n    98→        line = line.strip()\n    99→        if \":\" in line:\n   100→            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n   101→            if key:\n   102→                found_keys.add(key)\n   103→\n   104→    for field in required_fields:\n   105→        if field not in found_keys:\n   106→            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n   107→\n   108→    return True, None\n   109→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_018mjAB76tYjz2za4kiWML4f","session_id":"b2ef0efa-f51b-45bb-8b9b-bca7a80178ed","uuid":"67bf3a64-d5a8-4ea5-842f-d9be6b30801c"}

The monitor extracts task IDs,\ntool names, and file-change signals from these events to drive the TUI.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport re\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .debug_logger import debug_log\nfrom .models import MonitorState\n\n_dbg = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.sprint.debug.monitor\")\n\n# Patterns to extract from stringified event content\nTASK_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"T\\d{2}\\.\\d{2}\")\nTOOL_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(Read|Edit|MultiEdit|Write|Grep|Glob|Bash|TodoWrite|TodoRead|Task)\\b\"\n)\nFILES_CHANGED_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"(?:modified|created|edited|wrote|updated)\\s+[`'\\\"]?([^\\s`'\\\"]+\\.\\w+)\"\n)\n\n# Pattern for detecting budget exhaustion in NDJSON output\nERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"subtype\"\\s*:\\s*\"error_max_turns\"')\n\n\ndef detect_error_max_turns(output_path: Path) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if the last NDJSON line indicates budget exhaustion.\n\n    Scans the last non-empty line of the output file for the\n    ``\"subtype\":\"error_max_turns\"`` pattern, which signals that a\n    subprocess exhausted its turn budget.\n\n    Returns True if error_max_turns is detected, False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n        return False\n\n    if not content.strip():\n        return False\n\n    # Get last non-empty line\n    lines = content.strip().splitlines()\n    for line in reversed(lines):\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line:\n            return bool(ERROR_MAX_TURNS_PATTERN.search(line))\n\n    return False\n\n\n# Pattern for counting assistant message turns in NDJSON output\n# Each \"assistant\" type message represents one turn consumed.\n_TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"type\"\\s*:\\s*\"assistant\"')\n\n\ndef count_turns_from_output(output_path: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Extract the number of turns consumed from subprocess NDJSON output.\n\n    Counts lines containing ``\"type\":\"assistant\"`` which represent\n    individual assistant response turns. Each such line indicates\n    one turn was consumed from the budget.\n\n    Args:\n        output_path: Path to the subprocess NDJSON output file.\n\n    Returns:\n        Number of turns counted. Returns 0 if file is missing or empty.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n        return 0\n\n    if not content.strip():\n        return 0\n\n    count = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line and _TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN.search(line):\n            count += 1\n\n    return count\n\n\nclass OutputMonitor:\n    \"\"\"Background thread that watches a stream-json output file.\n\n    Reads incremental bytes, splits on newlines, and parses each complete\n    line as JSON.  Partial lines are buffered across poll cycles so no\n    data is lost when a write straddles a poll boundary.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, output_path: Path, poll_interval: float = 0.5):\n        self.output_path = output_path\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._thread: threading.Thread | None = None\n        self._last_read_pos: int = 0\n        self._line_buffer: str = \"\"\n        self._seen_files: set[str] = set()\n\n    def start(self):\n        \"\"\"Start the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(\n            target=self._poll_loop,\n            daemon=True,\n            name=\"output-monitor\",\n        )\n        self._thread.start()\n\n    def stop(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join(timeout=2.0)\n\n    def reset(self, new_output_path: Path):\n        \"\"\"Reset for a new phase (new output file).\"\"\"\n        self.output_path = new_output_path\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n\n    def _poll_loop(self):\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            self._poll_once()\n            self._stop_event.wait(self.poll_interval)\n\n    def _poll_once(self):\n        now = time.monotonic()\n\n        try:\n            size = self.output_path.stat().st_size\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return\n\n        self.state.output_bytes_prev = self.state.output_bytes\n        self.state.output_bytes = size\n\n        if size > self._last_read_pos:\n            # New data available — read incremental chunk\n            chunk = self._read_new_chunk(size)\n            if chunk:\n                self._process_chunk(chunk, now)\n        else:\n            # No growth — update stall counter\n            self.state.stall_seconds = now - self.state.last_event_time\n\n        debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n            \"output_file_stat\",\n            path=str(self.output_path),\n            size=size,\n            events_received=self.state.events_received,\n            last_event_time=round(self.state.last_event_time, 1),\n        )\n\n        # Growth rate: exponential moving average\n        delta = self.state.output_bytes - self.state.output_bytes_prev\n        alpha = 0.3\n        self.state.growth_rate_bps = (\n            alpha * (delta / self.poll_interval)\n            + (1 - alpha) * self.state.growth_rate_bps\n        )\n\n    def _read_new_chunk(self, current_size: int) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read only the bytes added since last poll.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(self.output_path, errors=\"replace\") as f:\n                f.seek(self._last_read_pos)\n                chunk = f.read(current_size - self._last_read_pos)\n                self._last_read_pos = current_size\n                return chunk\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _process_chunk(self, chunk: str, now: float):\n        \"\"\"Split chunk into lines, parse complete NDJSON lines, buffer partials.\"\"\"\n        # Prepend any leftover partial line from previous poll\n        data = self._line_buffer + chunk\n        lines = data.split(\"\\n\")\n\n        # Last element is either \"\" (if chunk ended with \\n) or a partial line\n        self._line_buffer = lines[-1]\n\n        # Process all complete lines (everything except the last split element)\n        for line in lines[:-1]:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n\n            # Update liveness on every complete line\n            self.state.last_growth_time = now\n            self.state.last_event_time = now\n            self.state.stall_seconds = 0.0\n            self.state.events_received += 1\n            self.state.lines_total += 1\n\n            # Try to parse as JSON\n            try:\n                event = json.loads(line)\n            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n                # Not valid JSON — still counts as a line for liveness,\n                # but try text-mode signal extraction as fallback\n                self._extract_signals_from_text(line)\n                continue\n\n            self._extract_signals_from_event(event)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_event(self, event: dict):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from a parsed NDJSON event.\"\"\"\n        event_type = event.get(\"type\", \"\")\n        debug_log(_dbg, \"ndjson_line_parsed\", event_type=event_type, parsed=True)\n\n        # Tool use events: extract tool name directly\n        if event_type == \"tool_use\":\n            tool = event.get(\"tool\", \"\")\n            if tool:\n                self.state.last_tool_used = tool\n\n        # Stringify the event for regex-based signal extraction\n        # This catches task IDs and file changes regardless of event structure\n        text = json.dumps(event, default=str)\n        self._extract_signals_from_text(text)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_text(self, text: str):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from text using regex.\"\"\"\n        # Last task ID (take the last match)\n        task_matches = TASK_ID_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if task_matches:\n            self.state.last_task_id = task_matches[-1]\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_extracted\", signal_type=\"task_id\", value=task_matches[-1])\n\n        # Last tool used (only if not already set by structured extraction)\n        tool_matches = TOOL_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if tool_matches:\n            self.state.last_tool_used = tool_matches[-1]\n            debug_log(_dbg, \"signal_extracted\", signal_type=\"tool_name\", value=tool_matches[-1])\n\n        # Files changed (accumulate unique paths)\n        file_matches = FILES_CHANGED_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        for f in file_matches:\n            self._seen_files.add(f)\n        self.state.files_changed = len(self._seen_files)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/process.py`\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport signal\nimport subprocess as _subprocess\nfrom typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Optional\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess as _PipelineClaudeProcess\n\nfrom .debug_logger import debug_log\nfrom .models import Phase, SprintConfig\n```\n\n### Class hierarchy\n- `ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess)`\n- `SignalHandler`\n- module functions for context injection\n\n### Key function signatures\n- `_make_spawn_hook(phase: Phase, config: SprintConfig)`\n- `_make_signal_hook(phase: Phase, config: SprintConfig)`\n- `_make_exit_hook(phase: Phase, config: SprintConfig)`\n- `class ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess)`\n  - `__init__(self, config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase)`\n  - `build_prompt(self) -> str`\n- `class SignalHandler`\n  - `install(self)`\n  - `uninstall(self)`\n  - `_handle(self, signum, frame)`\n- `build_task_context(prior_results: list[TaskResult], *, start_commit: str = \"\", compress_threshold: int = 3) -> str`\n- `get_git_diff_context(start_commit: str) -> str`\n- `compress_context_summary(results: list[TaskResult], *, keep_recent: int = 3) -> str`\n\n### Full source\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint process management — subprocess lifecycle, signal handling, and context injection.\n\nClaudeProcess extends pipeline.process.ClaudeProcess with sprint-specific\nconstructor (config, phase) and build_prompt(). was_timeout=(returncode == 124),\n        )\n\n    return on_exit\n\n\nclass ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess):\n    \"\"\"Sprint-specific claude process extending the pipeline base.\n\n    Defines only __init__ (with hook wiring) and build_prompt().\n    All subprocess lifecycle (start, wait, terminate) is inherited\n    from the pipeline base class; sprint debug logging is injected\n    via lifecycle hook factories.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig, phase: Phase):\n        self.config = config\n        self.phase = phase\n        prompt = output_format=\"stream-json\",\n            on_spawn=_make_spawn_hook(phase, config),\n            on_signal=_make_signal_hook(phase, config),\n            on_exit=_make_exit_hook(phase, config),\n        )\n\n    def build_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the /sc:task-unified prompt for this phase.\"\"\"\n        pn = self.phase.number\n        result_file = self.config.result_file(self.phase)\n        phase_file = self.phase.file\n\n        return (\n            f\"/sc:task-unified Execute all tasks in @{phase_file} \"\n            f\"--compliance strict --strategy systematic\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Execution Rules\\n\"\n            f\"- Execute tasks in order (T{pn:02d}XX.01, T{pn:02d}XX.02, etc.)\\n\"\n            f\"- For STRICT tier tasks: use Sequential MCP for analysis, \"\n            f\"run quality verification\\n\"\n            f\"- For STANDARD tier tasks: run direct test execution per \"\n            f\"acceptance criteria\\n\"\n            f\"- For LIGHT tier tasks: quick sanity check only\\n\"\n            f\"- For EXEMPT tier tasks: skip formal verification\\n\"\n            f\"- If a STRICT-tier task fails, STOP and report -- \"\n            f\"do not continue to next task\\n\"\n            f\"- For all other tier failures, log the failure and continue\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Completion Protocol\\n\"\n            f\"When ALL tasks in this phase are complete \"\n            f\"(or halted on STRICT failure):\\n\"\n            f\"1. Write a phase completion report to {result_file} containing:\\n\"\n            f\"   - YAML frontmatter with: phase, status (PASS|FAIL|PARTIAL), \"\n            f\"tasks_total, tasks_passed, tasks_failed\\n\"\n            f\"   - Per-task status table: Task ID, Title, Tier, Status \"\n            f\"(pass/fail/skip), Evidence\\n\"\n            f\"   - Files modified (list all paths)\\n\"\n            f\"   - Blockers for next phase (if any)\\n\"\n            f\"   - The literal string EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE \"\n            f\"or EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\\n\"\n            f\"2. If any task produced file changes, list them under \"\n            f\"## Files Modified\\n\"\n            f\"\\n\"\n            f\"## Important\\n\"\n            f\"- This is Phase {pn} of a multi-phase sprint\\n\"\n            f\"- Previous phases have already been executed in separate sessions\\n\"\n            f\"- Do not re-execute work from prior phases\\n\"\n            f\"- Focus only on the tasks defined in the phase file\"\n        )\n\n\nclass SignalHandler:\n    \"\"\"Register signal handlers for graceful sprint shutdown.\n\n    On SIGINT/SIGTERM:\n    1. Set the shutdown flag (checked by the executor loop)\n    2. The executor terminates the current claude process\n    3. Writes partial execution log\n    4. Exits with appropriate code\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.shutdown_requested = False\n        self._original_sigint = None\n        self._original_sigterm = None\n\n    def install(self):\n        \"\"\"Install signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        self._original_sigint = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGINT)\n        self._original_sigterm = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle)\n\n    def uninstall(self):\n        \"\"\"Restore original signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        if self._original_sigint is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._original_sigint)\n        if self._original_sigterm is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._original_sigterm)\n\n    def _handle(self, signum, frame):\n        self.shutdown_requested = True\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Context Injection\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef build_task_context(\n    prior_results: list[TaskResult],\n    *,\n    start_commit: str = \"\",\n    compress_threshold: int = 3,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build deterministic context from prior task results for injection into prompts.\n\n    Produces structured markdown containing:\n    - Prior task results (status, gate outcome, key details)\n    - Gate outcomes (pass/fail/deferred per prior task)\n    - Remediation history (reimbursement amounts)\n    - Git diff summary (if start_commit is provided)\n\n    Context is compressed via progressive summarization when the number of\n    prior results exceeds ``compress_threshold`` (default 3): older tasks\n    are reduced to a one-line summary while recent tasks retain full detail.\n\n    Args:\n        prior_results: Ordered list of TaskResult from preceding tasks.\n        start_commit: Git commit ref for diff context (empty = skip).\n        compress_threshold: Number of recent tasks to keep at full detail.\n\n    Returns:\n        Structured markdown string for inclusion in task prompts.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not prior_results:\n        return \"\"\n\n    sections: list[str] = [\"## Prior Task Context\\n\"]\n\n    # Apply progressive summarization\n    if len(prior_results) > compress_threshold:\n        compressed = compress_context_summary(prior_results, keep_recent=compress_threshold)\n        sections.append(compressed)\n    else:\n        for result in prior_results:\n            sections.append(result.to_context_summary(verbose=True))\n            sections.append(\"\")\n\n    # Gate outcome summary\n    sections.append(\"\\n### Gate Outcomes\\n\")\n    for result in prior_results:\n        sections.append(\n            f\"- {result.task.task_id}: {result.gate_outcome.value}\"\n        )\n\n    # Remediation history (only tasks with reimbursement)\n    remediated = [r for r in prior_results if r.reimbursement_amount > 0]\n    if remediated:\n        sections.append(\"\\n### Remediation History\\n\")\n        for result in remediated:\n            sections.append(\n                f\"- {result.task.task_id}: reimbursed {result.reimbursement_amount} turns\"\n            )\n\n    # Git diff context\n    if start_commit:\n        diff_section = get_git_diff_context(start_commit)\n        if diff_section:\n            sections.append(f\"\\n{diff_section}\")\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(sections) + \"\\n\"\n\n\ndef get_git_diff_context(start_commit: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Run ``git diff --stat`` from start_commit and return structured section.\n\n    Args:\n        start_commit: Git ref (SHA or branch) marking the sprint start.\n\n    Returns:\n        Markdown section with the diff summary, or empty string on error.\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        result = _subprocess.run(\n            [\"git\", \"diff\", \"--stat\", start_commit],\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            timeout=10,\n        )\n        if result.returncode != 0 or not result.stdout.strip():\n            return \"\"\n        return (\n            \"### Git Changes Since Sprint Start\\n\\n\"\n            \"```\\n\"\n            f\"{result.stdout.strip()}\\n\"\n            \"```\"\n        )\n    except (FileNotFoundError, _subprocess.TimeoutExpired, OSError):\n        return \"\"\n\n\ndef compress_context_summary(\n    results: list[TaskResult],\n    *,\n    keep_recent: int = 3,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Compress older task context while preserving recent detail.\n\n    Tasks beyond the ``keep_recent`` window are reduced to a one-line\n    summary (status + gate outcome). Recent tasks retain full detail.\n    This bounds context growth for long sprints.\n\n    Args:\n        results: All prior TaskResult objects in execution order.\n        keep_recent: Number of most recent tasks to keep at full detail.\n\n    Returns:\n        Markdown string with compressed older tasks and detailed recent tasks.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not results:\n        return \"\"\n\n    lines: list[str] = []\n\n    # Compressed older tasks\n    if len(results) > keep_recent:\n        older = results[:-keep_recent]\n        lines.append(\"#### Earlier Tasks (compressed)\\n\")\n        for result in older:\n            lines.append(result.to_context_summary(verbose=False))\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    # Recent tasks at full detail\n    recent = results[-keep_recent:]\n    lines.append(\"#### Recent Tasks\\n\")\n    for result in recent:\n        lines.append(result.to_context_summary(verbose=True))\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/tui.py`\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport None\n        self.monitor_state = MonitorState()\n        self.current_phase: Optional[Phase] = None\n        self._live: Optional[Live] = None\n        self._live_failed: bool = False\n        #      self._show_gate_column: bool = getattr(config, \"grace_period\", 0) > 0  # silences future updates after first render error\n\n    def start(self) -> Live:\n        \"\"\"Start the Live display and return it for the executor to use.\"\"\"\n        debug_log(_dbg, \"tui_start\")\n        self._live = Live(\n            self._render(),\n            console=self.console,\n            refresh_per_second=2,\n            screen=False,\n        )\n        self._live.start()\n        return self._live\n\n    def stop(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop the Live display.\"\"\"\n        debug_log(_dbg, \"tui_stop\")\n        if self._live:\n            self._live.stop()\n\n    def update(\n        self,\n        sprint_result: SprintResult,\n        monitor_state: MonitorState,\n        current_phase: Optional[Phase],\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Called by the executor to refresh the display.\n\n        Rendering errors (terminal resize race, broken pipe, etc.) are caught\n        so a display glitch cannot abort the running sprint.\n        \"\"\"\n        self.sprint_result = sprint_result\n        self.monitor_state = monitor_state\n        self.current_phase = current_phase\n        if self._live and not self._live_failed:\n            try:\n                self._live.update(self._render())\n                debug_log(\n                    _dbg,\n                    \"tui_update\",\n                    events_received=monitor_state.events_received,\n                    stall_status=monitor_state.stall_status,\n                    last_event_time=round(monitor_state.last_event_time, 1),\n                )\n            except Exception as exc:\n                import sys\n                self._live_failed = True\n                debug_log(_dbg, \"tui_live_failed\", error=str(exc), error_type=type(exc).__name__)\n                print(f\"[TUI] Display error (continuing sprint): {exc}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    def _render(self) -> Panel:\n        \"\"\"Build the complete TUI layout.\"\"\"\n        header = self._build_header()\n        table = self._build_phase_table()\n        progress = self._build_progress()\n        detail = self._build_active_panel()\n\n        body = RichGroup(header, \"\", table, \"\", progress, \"\", detail)\n\n        return Panel(\n            body,\n            title=\"[bold]SUPERCLAUDE SPRINT RUNNER[/]\",\n            border_style=\"blue\",\n            padding=(1, 2),\n        )\n\n    def _build_header(self) -> Text:\n        elapsed = self.sprint_result.duration_display if self.sprint_result else \"0s\"\n        index_name = self.config.index_path.parent.name\n        return Text.from_markup(\n            f\"[dim]{index_name}[/]    Elapsed: [bold]{elapsed}[/]\"\n        )\n\n    def _build_phase_table(self) -> Table:\n        table = Table(\n            show_header=True,\n            header_style=\"bold\",\n            border_style=\"dim\",\n            pad_edge=False,\n            box=None,\n        )\n        table.add_column(\"#\", width=3, justify=\"right\")\n        table.add_column(\"Phase\", min_width=30)\n        table.add_column(\"Status\", width=12, justify=\"center\")\n        if self._show_gate_column:\n            table.add_column(\"Gate\", width=6, justify=\"center\")\n        table.add_column(\"Duration\", width=10, justify=\"right\")\n        table.add_column(\"Tasks\", width=8, justify=\"center\")\n\n        if not self.sprint_result:\n            return table\n\n        for phase in self.config.active_phases:\n            result = next(\n                (\n                    r\n                    for r in self.sprint_result.phase_results\n                    if r.phase.number == phase.number\n                ),\n                None,\n            )\n            status = result.status if result else PhaseStatus.PENDING\n            if phase == self.current_phase and not (result and result.status.is_terminal):\n                status = PhaseStatus.RUNNING\n\n            style = STATUS_STYLES[status]\n            duration = (\n                result.duration_display\n                if result and status.is_terminal\n                else (\n                    f\"{int(self.monitor_state.stall_seconds)}s\"\n                    if status == PhaseStatus.RUNNING\n                    else \"-\"\n                )\n            )\n            tasks = f\"{result.last_task_id}\" if result else \"-\"\n\n            row: list[str] = [\n                str(phase.number),\n                phase.display_name,\n                STATUS_ICONS.get(status, str(status.value)),\n            ]\n            if self._show_gate_column:\n                gate_state = self.gate_states.get(phase.number, GateDisplayState.NONE)\n                row.append(gate_state.icon)\n            row.extend([duration, tasks])\n\n            table.add_row(\n                *row,\n                style=style if status == PhaseStatus.PENDING else \"\",\n            )\n\n        return table\n\n    def _build_progress(self) -> Progress:\n        \"\"\"Overall sprint progress bar.\"\"\"\n        progress = Progress(\n            TextColumn(\"[bold]Progress\"),\n            BarColumn(bar_width=40),\n            TextColumn(\"{task.percentage:>3.0f}%\"),\n            TextColumn(\"[dim]{task.completed}/{task.total} tasks[/]\"),\n        )\n        total = len(self.config.active_phases)\n        done = self.sprint_result.phases_passed if self.sprint_result else 0\n        progress.add_task(\"sprint\", total=total, completed=done)\n        return progress\n\n    def _build_active_panel(self) -> Panel:\n        \"\"\"Detail panel for the currently-running phase.\"\"\"\n        if not self.current_phase:\n            # Terminal state\n            if self.sprint_result and self.sprint_result.finished_at:\n                return self._build_terminal_panel()\n            return Panel(\"[dim]Waiting...[/]\", title=\"Active Phase\")\n\n        ms = self.monitor_state\n        stall_display = ms.stall_status\n        stall_style = (\n            \"bold red blink\"\n            if stall_display == \"STALLED\"\n            else \"yellow\"\n            if stall_display == \"thinking...\"\n            else \"green\"\n        )\n\n        lines = [\n            f\"File:    {self.current_phase.basename}\",\n            f\"Status:  RUNNING -- [{stall_style}]{stall_display}[/]\",\n            \"\",\n            f\"Last task:     {ms.last_task_id or '-'}\",\n            f\"Last tool:     {ms.last_tool_used or '-'}\",\n            f\"Output size:   {ms.output_size_display}  (+{ms.growth_rate_bps:.1f} B/s)\",\n            f\"Files changed: {ms.files_changed}\",\n        ]\n\n        return Panel(\n            \"\\n\".join(lines),\n            title=f\"[bold yellow]ACTIVE: Phase {self.current_phase.number}[/]\",\n            border_style=\"yellow\",\n        )\n\n    def _build_terminal_panel(self) -> Panel:\n        \"\"\"Build the final state panel (complete or halted).\"\"\"\n        sr = self.sprint_result\n        if sr.outcome.value == \"success\":\n            content = (\n                f\"Result:  [bold green]ALL PHASES PASSED[/]\\n\"\n                f\"Log:     {self.config.execution_log_md}\"\n            )\n            return Panel(content, title=\"[bold green]Sprint Complete[/]\", border_style=\"green\")\n        else:\n            lines = [\n                f\"Result:  [bold red]{sr.outcome.value.upper()}[/]\"\n            ]\n            if sr.halt_phase:\n                lines.append(f\"Halted at Phase {sr.halt_phase}\")\n            resume = sr.resume_command()\n            if resume:\n                lines.append(f\"\\nResume:  [bold]{resume}[/]\")\n            content = \"\\n\".join(lines)\n            return Panel(content, title=\"[bold red]Sprint Halted[/]\", border_style=\"red\")\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/logging_.py`\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport
