rich.console import Console\n\nfrom .models import PhaseResult, PhaseStatus, SprintConfig, SprintResult\n\n\nclass SprintLogger:\n    \"\"\"Dual-format sprint logger: JSONL (machine) + Markdown (human).\n\n    Log levels:\n    - DEBUG: JSONL file only\n    - INFO:  JSONL + Markdown + screen\n    - WARN:  JSONL + Markdown + screen (highlighted)\n    - ERROR: JSONL + Markdown + screen (highlighted) + terminal bell\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig):\n        self.config = config\n        self.console = Console(stderr=True)  # TUI uses stdout\n        config.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    def write_header(self, sprint: SprintResult):\n        \"\"\"Write sprint header to both log formats.\"\"\"\n        # JSONL\n        self._jsonl(\n            {\n                \"event\": \"sprint_start\",\n                \"timestamp\": sprint.started_at.isoformat(),\n                \"index\": str(self.config.index_path),\n                \"phases\": f\"{self.config.start_phase}-\"\n                f\"{self.config.end_phase or 'last'}\",\n                \"max_turns\": self.config.max_turns,\n                \"model\": self.config.model or \"default\",\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Markdown\n        end = self.config.end_phase or max(p.number for p in self.config.phases)\n        md = [\n            \"# Sprint Execution Log\",\n            \"\",\n            f\"**Started**: {sprint.started_at.isoformat()}\",\n            f\"**Index**: {self.config.index_path}\",\n            f\"**Phases**: {self.config.start_phase}--{end}\",\n            f\"**Max turns**: {self.config.max_turns}\",\n            f\"**Model**: {self.config.model or 'default'}\",\n            \"\",\n            \"| Phase | Status | Started | Completed | Duration | Exit |\",\n            \"|-------|--------|---------|-----------|----------|------|\",\n        ]\n        self.config.execution_log_md.write_text(\"\\n\".join(md) + \"\\n\")\n\n    def write_phase_start(self, phase, started_at):\n        \"\"\"Log phase start transition (RUNNING).\"\"\"\n        self._jsonl(\n            {\n                \"event\": \"phase_start\",\n                \"phase\": phase.number,\n                \"phase_name\": phase.display_name,\n                \"phase_file\": str(phase.file),\n                \"timestamp\": started_at.isoformat(),\n            }\n        )\n\n    def write_phase_interrupt(self, phase, started_at, finished_at, exit_code: int):\n        \"\"\"Log a phase that was interrupted mid-execution by a signal.\n\n        Balances the phase_start event so the JSONL log has a closing event\n        for every opening phase_start event.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._jsonl(\n            {\n                \"event\": \"phase_interrupt\",\n                \"phase\": phase.number,\n                \"phase_name\": phase.display_name,\n                \"started_at\": started_at.isoformat(),\n                \"interrupted_at\": finished_at.isoformat(),\n                \"duration_seconds\": (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds(),\n                \"exit_code\": exit_code,\n            }\n        )\n\n    def write_phase_result(self, result: PhaseResult):\n        \"\"\"Log a phase completion.\"\"\"\n        # JSONL (all severities)\n        self._jsonl(\n            {\n                \"event\": \"phase_complete\",\n                \"phase\": result.phase.number,\n                \"phase_name\": result.phase.display_name,\n                \"status\": result.status.value,\n                \"exit_code\": result.exit_code,\n                \"started_at\": result.started_at.isoformat(),\n                \"finished_at\": result.finished_at.isoformat(),\n                \"duration_seconds\": result.duration_seconds,\n                \"output_bytes\": result.output_bytes,\n                \"error_bytes\": result.error_bytes,\n                \"last_task_id\": result.last_task_id,\n                \"files_changed\": result.files_changed,\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Markdown (INFO/WARN/ERROR only; DEBUG omitted)\n        if result.status != PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL:\n            row = (\n                f\"| Phase {result.phase.number} \"\n                f\"| {result.status.value} \"\n                f\"| {result.started_at.isoformat()} \"\n                f\"| {result.finished_at.isoformat()} \"\n                f\"| {result.duration_display} \"\n                f\"| {result.exit_code} |\"\n            )\n            with open(self.config.execution_log_md, \"a\") as f:\n                f.write(row + \"\\n\")\n\n        # Screen severity routing\n        # DEBUG -> JSONL only (PASS_NO_SIGNAL)\n        # INFO  -> screen + JSONL (PASS/PASS_NO_REPORT)\n        # WARN  -> highlighted stderr + JSONL (HALT/TIMEOUT)\n        # ERROR -> highlighted stderr + JSONL + bell (ERROR)\n        if result.status == PhaseStatus.ERROR:\n            self._screen_error(\n                f\"Phase {result.phase.number}: {result.status.value} \"\n                f\"({result.duration_display})\"\n            )\n        elif result.status in (PhaseStatus.HALT, PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT):\n            self._screen_warn(\n                f\"Phase {result.phase.number}: {result.status.value} \"\n                f\"({result.duration_display})\"\n            )\n        elif result.status in (PhaseStatus.PASS, PhaseStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT):\n            self._screen_info(\n                f\"Phase {result.phase.number}: {result.status.value} \"\n                f\"({result.duration_display})\"\n            )\n\n    def write_summary(self, sprint: SprintResult):\n        \"\"\"Write sprint summary to both logs.\"\"\"\n        self._jsonl(\n            {\n                \"event\": \"sprint_complete\",\n                \"outcome\": sprint.outcome.value,\n                \"duration_seconds\": sprint.duration_seconds,\n                \"phases_passed\": sprint.phases_passed,\n                \"phases_failed\": sprint.phases_failed,\n                \"halt_phase\": sprint.halt_phase,\n            }\n        )\n\n        with open(self.config.execution_log_md, \"a\") as f:\n            f.write(f\"\\n**Outcome**: {sprint.outcome.value}\\n\")\n            f.write(f\"**Total duration**: {sprint.duration_display}\\n\")\n            if sprint.halt_phase:\n                f.write(f\"**Halted at**: Phase {sprint.halt_phase}\\n\")\n                f.write(f\"**Resume**: `{sprint.resume_command()}`\\n\")\n\n    def _jsonl(self, data: dict):\n        with open(self.config.execution_log_jsonl, \"a\") as f:\n            f.write(json.dumps(data, default=str) + \"\\n\")\n\n    def _screen_info(self, msg: str):\n        self.console.print(f\"[green][INFO][/] {msg}\")\n\n    def _screen_warn(self, msg: str):\n        self.console.print(f\"[yellow][WARN][/] {msg}\")\n\n    def _screen_error(self, msg: str):\n        self.console.print(f\"[bold red][ERROR][/] {msg}\\a\")  # \\a = terminal bell\n\n\ndef read_status_from_log():\n    \"\"\"Read and display sprint status from execution log. (Stub)\"\"\"\n    import click\n\n    click.echo(\"Status command not yet connected to active sprint.\")\n\n\ndef tail_log(lines: int = 50, follow: bool = False):\n    \"\"\"Tail the execution log. (Stub)\"\"\"\n    import click\n\n    click.echo(\"Logs command not yet connected to active sprint.\")\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/diagnostics.py`\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom .models import MonitorState, Phase, PhaseResult, PhaseStatus, SprintConfig\n```\n\n### Class hierarchy\n- `FailureCategory(Enum)`\n- `DiagnosticBundle`\n- `DiagnosticCollector`\n- `FailureClassifier`\n- `ReportGenerator`\n\n### Key function signatures\n- `class FailureCategory(Enum)`\n- `@dataclass class DiagnosticBundle`\n  - `summary(self) -> str`\n- `class DiagnosticCollector`\n  - `__init__(self, config: SprintConfig)`\n  - `collect(self, phase: Phase, phase_result: PhaseResult, monitor_state: MonitorState) -> DiagnosticBundle`\n  - `_read_phase_debug_entries(self, phase: Phase) -> list[str]`\n  - `_read_tail(self, path: Path, lines: int = 10) -> str`\n- `class FailureClassifier`\n  - `classify(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> FailureCategory`\n- `class ReportGenerator`\n  - `generate(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> str`\n  - `write(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle, output_path: Path) -> None`\n\n### Full source\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint diagnostics — failure classification, bundle collection, and reporting.\n\nProvides post-failure analysis by collecting debug logs, process state,\nand output artifacts into a structured diagnostic bundle. The failure\nclassifier assigns a category (stall, timeout, crash, error, unknown) based\non evidence from the debug log and process exit state.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom .models import MonitorState, Phase, PhaseResult, PhaseStatus, SprintConfig\n\n\nclass FailureCategory(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Classification of phase failure root cause.\"\"\"\n\n    STALL = \"stall\"\n    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"\n    CRASH = \"crash\"\n    ERROR = \"error\"\n    UNKNOWN = \"unknown\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass DiagnosticBundle:\n    \"\"\"Structured collection of diagnostic evidence for a failed phase.\n\n    Contains everything needed to understand why a phase failed:\n    debug log entries, process state, output samples, and classified\n    failure category.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    phase: Phase\n    phase_result: PhaseResult\n    category: FailureCategory = FailureCategory.UNKNOWN\n    debug_log_entries: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    last_events: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    output_tail: str = \"\"\n    stderr_tail: str = \"\"\n    monitor_state_snapshot: Optional[dict] = None\n    watchdog_triggered: bool = False\n    stall_duration: float = 0.0\n    classification_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def summary(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"One-line summary of the diagnosis.\"\"\"\n        return (\n            f\"Phase {self.phase.number}: {self.category.value} \"\n            f\"(exit={self.phase_result.exit_code}, \"\n            f\"duration={self.phase_result.duration_display})\"\n        )\n\n\nclass DiagnosticCollector:\n    \"\"\"Collects diagnostic evidence from various sources into a bundle.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig):\n        self.config = config\n\n    def collect(\n        self,\n        phase: Phase,\n        phase_result: PhaseResult,\n        monitor_state: MonitorState,\n    ) -> DiagnosticBundle:\n        \"\"\"Collect all available diagnostic data for a failed phase.\n\n        Reads the debug log (if enabled), output and error files, and\n        captures the monitor state snapshot.\n        \"\"\"\n        bundle = DiagnosticBundle(\n            phase=phase,\n            phase_result=phase_result,\n        )\n\n        # Capture monitor state\n        bundle.monitor_state_snapshot = {\n            \"output_bytes\": monitor_state.output_bytes,\n            \"events_received\": monitor_state.events_received,\n            \"stall_seconds\": monitor_state.stall_seconds,\n            \"stall_status\": monitor_state.stall_status,\n            \"last_task_id\": monitor_state.last_task_id,\n            \"last_tool_used\": monitor_state.last_tool_used,\n            \"files_changed\": monitor_state.files_changed,\n            \"growth_rate_bps\": monitor_state.growth_rate_bps,\n        }\n        bundle.stall_duration = monitor_state.stall_seconds\n\n        # Read debug log entries for this phase\n        if self.config.debug and self.config.debug_log_path.exists():\n            bundle.debug_log_entries = self._read_phase_debug_entries(phase)\n            bundle.watchdog_triggered = any(\n                \"watchdog_triggered\" in entry\n                for entry in bundle.debug_log_entries\n            )\n            # Extract last N events\n            bundle.last_events = bundle.debug_log_entries[-20:]\n\n        # Read output tail\n        output_file = self.config.output_file(phase)\n        if output_file.exists():\n            bundle.output_tail = self._read_tail(output_file, lines=10)\n\n        # Read stderr tail\n        error_file = self.config.error_file(phase)\n        if error_file.exists():\n            bundle.stderr_tail = self._read_tail(error_file, lines=10)\n\n        return bundle\n\n    def _read_phase_debug_entries(self, phase: Phase) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Read debug log entries relevant to this phase.\"\"\"\n        entries = []\n        in_phase = False\n        try:\n            for line in self.config.debug_log_path.open():\n                line = line.rstrip()\n                if not line or line.startswith(\"#\"):\n                    continue\n                if \"PHASE_BEGIN\" in line and f\"phase={phase.number}\" in line:\n                    in_phase = True\n                if in_phase:\n                    entries.append(line)\n                if \"PHASE_END\" in line and f\"phase={phase.number}\" in line:\n                    break\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n        return entries\n\n    def _read_tail(self, path: Path, lines: int = 10) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read the last N lines of a file.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            all_lines = path.read_text(errors=\"replace\").splitlines()\n            return \"\\n\".join(all_lines[-lines:])\n        except OSError:\n            return \"\"\n\n\nclass FailureClassifier:\n    \"\"\"Classifies phase failures based on diagnostic evidence.\"\"\"\n\n    def classify(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> FailureCategory:\n        \"\"\"Determine the most likely failure category.\n\n        Classification priority:\n        1. STALL: watchdog triggered or high stall duration\n        2. TIMEOUT: exit code 124 (process timeout)\n        3. CRASH: non-zero exit with low stall (subprocess died)\n        4. ERROR: result file indicates error/halt\n        5. UNKNOWN: no clear evidence\n        \"\"\"\n        evidence = []\n        status = bundle.phase_result.status\n        exit_code = bundle.phase_result.exit_code\n\n        # 1. Stall detection\n        if bundle.watchdog_triggered:\n            evidence.append(\"Watchdog triggered — stall detected by timeout\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.STALL\n\n        if bundle.stall_duration > 120:\n            evidence.append(f\"High stall duration: {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.STALL\n\n        # 2. Timeout\n        if status == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT or exit_code == 124:\n            evidence.append(f\"Process timeout (exit code {exit_code})\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.TIMEOUT\n\n        # 3. Crash (non-zero exit, not timeout, low stall)\n        if exit_code != 0 and bundle.stall_duration < 30:\n            evidence.append(f\"Process crash (exit code {exit_code}, stall {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s)\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.CRASH\n\n        # 4. Error (result file signals)\n        if status in (PhaseStatus.HALT, PhaseStatus.ERROR):\n            evidence.append(f\"Phase status: {status.value}\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.ERROR\n\n        # 5. Unknown\n        evidence.append(\"No clear failure pattern identified\")\n        bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n        return FailureCategory.UNKNOWN\n\n\nclass ReportGenerator:\n    \"\"\"Generates human-readable diagnostic reports.\"\"\"\n\n    def generate(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a structured diagnostic report from a bundle.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            f\"# Diagnostic Report — Phase {bundle.phase.number}\",\n            \"\",\n            \"## Summary\",\n            f\"- **Category**: {bundle.category.value}\",\n            f\"- **Status**: {bundle.phase_result.status.value}\",\n            f\"- **Exit Code**: {bundle.phase_result.exit_code}\",\n            f\"- **Duration**: {bundle.phase_result.duration_display}\",\n            f\"- **Stall Duration**: {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s\",\n            f\"- **Watchdog Triggered**: {bundle.watchdog_triggered}\",\n            \"\",\n            \"## Evidence\",\n        ]\n\n        for ev in bundle.classification_evidence:\n            lines.append(f\"- {ev}\")\n\n        if bundle.monitor_state_snapshot:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Monitor State\")\n            for k, v in bundle.monitor_state_snapshot.items():\n                lines.append(f\"- **{k}**: {v}\")\n\n        if bundle.last_events:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Last Debug Events\")\n            lines.append(\"```\")\n            for entry in bundle.last_events[-10:]:\n                lines.append(entry)\n            lines.append(\"```\")\n\n        if bundle.output_tail:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Output Tail\")\n            lines.append(\"```\")\n            lines.append(bundle.output_tail)\n            lines.append(\"```\")\n\n        if bundle.stderr_tail:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Stderr Tail\")\n            lines.append(\"```\")\n            lines.append(bundle.stderr_tail)\n            lines.append(\"```\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def write(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle, output_path: Path) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Write diagnostic report to file.\"\"\"\n        output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        report = self.generate(bundle)\n        output_path.write_text(report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/models.py`\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ self.started_at).total_seconds()\n\n\nclass DeliverableKind(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Classification of deliverable type for decomposition and analysis.\"\"\"\n\n    IMPLEMENT = \"implement\"\n    VERIFY = \"verify\"\n    INVARIANT_CHECK = \"invariant_check\"\n    FMEA_TEST = \"fmea_test\"\n    GUARD_TEST = \"guard_test\"\n    CONTRACT_TEST = \"contract_test\"\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_str(cls, value: str) -> DeliverableKind:\n        \"\"\"Parse a string into a DeliverableKind, raising ValueError on unknown.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return cls(value)\n        except ValueError:\n            valid = \", \".join(k.value for k in cls)\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Unknown deliverable kind: {value!r}. Valid kinds: {valid}\"\n            )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Deliverable:\n    \"\"\"A single deliverable within a pipeline step or roadmap task.\n\n    Attributes:\n        id: Unique identifier (e.g. 'D-0001', 'D-0001.a').\n        description: Human-readable description of what this deliverable produces.\n        kind: Classification for decomposition passes. Defaults to 'implement'\n              for backward compatibility with pre-extension deliverables.\n        metadata: Attachment point for analytical passes (M2-M4). Defaults to\n                  empty dict. Pre-extension dicts without 'kind' default to 'implement'.\"\"\"\n        kind_str = data.get(\"kind\", \"implement\")\n        return cls(\n            id=data[\"id\"],\n            description=data[\"description\"],\n            kind=DeliverableKind.from_str(kind_str),\n            metadata=data.get(\"metadata\", {}),\n        )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass PipelineConfig:\n    \"\"\"Configuration shared by both sprint and roadmap pipelines.\"\"\"\n\n    work_dir: Path = field(default_factory=lambda: Path(\".\"))\n    dry_run: bool = False\n    max_turns: int = 100\n    model: str = \"\"\n    permission_flag: str = \"--dangerously-skip-permissions\"\n    debug: bool = False\n    grace_period: int = 0\n```\n\n### Portify-relevant pattern notes\n- `PipelineConfig` is intentionally minimal and reused by sprint/roadmap.\n- `GateCriteria.enforcement_tier` uses `Literal[...]`, not an Enum.\n- `StepResult` has timing fields and derived `duration_seconds`, but no exit-code/output fields; those are extended in sprint-level result models.\n\n---\n\n## 7) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py`\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n```\n\n### Key requested function\n- `gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]`\n\n### Full source\n```python\n\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n\nValidates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n\nNFR-003: No subprocess import. NFR-007: No sprint/roadmap imports.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\ndef gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    \"\"\"Validate a step's output against its gate criteria.\n\n    Returns (True, None) on pass.\n    Returns (False, reason) on failure where reason is human-readable.\n    \"\"\"\n    tier = criteria.enforcement_tier\n\n    # EXEMPT: always passes\n    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    if not output_file.exists():\n        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n\n    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n\n    # LIGHT stops here\n    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n        return False, (\n            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n            f\"in {output_file}\"\n        )\n\n    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file)\n        if not ok:\n            return False, reason\n\n    # STANDARD stops here\n    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n            if not check.check_fn(content):\n                return False, f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\"\n\n    return True, None\n\n\n_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(\n    r\"^---[ \\t]*\\n((?:[ \\t]*\\w[\\w\\s]*:.*\\n)+)---[ \\t]*$\",\n    re.MULTILINE,\n)\n\n\ndef _check_frontmatter(\n    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\n\n    Uses regex with re.MULTILINE to discover frontmatter anywhere in the\n    document (not just byte 0), allowing conversational preamble before the\n    ``---`` delimiters.  Horizontal rules (``---`` with no ``key: value``\n    content) are rejected because the pattern requires at least one\n    ``key: value`` line between delimiters.\n    \"\"\"\n    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n    if match is None:\n        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter not found in {output_file}\"\n\n    frontmatter_text = match.group(1)\n\n    # Extract keys from key: value lines\n    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found_keys.add(key)\n\n    for field in required_fields:\n        if field not in found_keys:\n            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n\n    return True, None\n```\n\n### Portify-relevant pattern notes\n- Tier progression is strictly layered: `EXEMPT -> LIGHT -> STANDARD -> STRICT`.\n- `STRICT` only adds semantic checks; it does not alter earlier checks.\n- Frontmatter detection is regex-based, not YAML-parser-based.\n\n---\n\n## 8) `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/sprint/models.py`\n\nThis file is long, but the tool returned the full file, so below is the full implementation.\n\n### Imports\n```python\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import (\n    PipelineConfig,\n    Step,\n    StepResult,\n    StepStatus,\n)\n```\n\n### Class hierarchy\n- `TaskEntry`\n- `TaskStatus(Enum)`\n- `GateOutcome(Enum)`\n- `GateDisplayState(Enum)`\n- `TaskResult`\n- `PhaseStatus(Enum)`\n- `SprintOutcome(Enum)`\n- `Phase`\n- `SprintConfig(PipelineConfig)`\n- `SprintStep(Step)`\n- `PhaseResult(StepResult)`\n- `SprintResult`\n- `MonitorState`\n- `TurnLedger`\n- `ShadowGateMetrics`\n\n### Key requested type\n- `TurnLedger`\n\n### Full source\n```python\n\"\"\"Sprint data models — enums, dataclasses, and pure-data types.\n\nAll other sprint modules depend on these types. 60}s\"\n        return f\"{s // 3600}h {(s % 3600) // 60}m\"\n\n    @property\n    def phases_passed(self) -> int:\n        return sum(1 for r in self.phase_results if r.status.is_success)\n\n    @property\n    def phases_failed(self) -> int:\n        return sum(1 for r in self.phase_results if r.status.is_failure)\n\n    def resume_command(self) -> str:\n        if self.halt_phase is not None:\n            end = self.config.end_phase or max(\n                p.number for p in self.config.phases\n            )\n            return (\n                f\"superclaude sprint run {self.config.index_path} \"\n                f\"--start {self.halt_phase} --end {end}\"\n            )\n        return \"\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass MonitorState:\n    \"\"\"Real-time state extracted by the sidecar monitor thread.\n\n    With stream-json output, liveness is tracked via ``last_event_time``\n    (updated on each parsed NDJSON line) rather than raw byte growth.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    output_bytes: int = 0\n    output_bytes_prev: int = 0\n    last_growth_time: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)\n    last_event_time: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)\n    phase_started_at: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)\n    events_received: int = 0\n    last_task_id: str = \"\"\n    last_tool_used: str = \"\"\n    files_changed: int = 0\n    lines_total: int = 0\n    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0  # bytes per second\n    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n\n    @property\n    def stall_status(self) -> str:\n        now = time.monotonic()\n        if self.events_received == 0:\n            # No events yet — subprocess is starting up\n            if now - self.phase_started_at > 120:\n                return \"STALLED\"\n            return \"waiting...\"\n        # Events have been flowing — check time since last event\n        since_last = now - self.last_event_time\n        if since_last > 120:\n            return \"STALLED\"\n        if since_last > 30:\n            return \"thinking...\"\n        return \"active\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_size_display(self) -> str:\n        if self.output_bytes < 1024:\n            return f\"{self.output_bytes} B\"\n        if self.output_bytes < 1024 * 1024:\n            return f\"{self.output_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB\"\n        return f\"{self.output_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TurnLedger:\n    \"\"\"Economic model for subprocess turn budget tracking.\n\n    Tracks budget allocation, consumption, and reimbursement for sprint\n    subprocesses. Enforces monotonicity: consumed can only increase.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    initial_budget: int\n    consumed: int = 0\n    reimbursed: int = 0\n    reimbursement_rate: float = 0.8\n    minimum_allocation: int = 5\n    minimum_remediation_budget: int = 3\n\n    def available(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Return available turns: initial_budget - consumed + reimbursed.\"\"\"\n        return self.initial_budget - self.consumed + self.reimbursed\n\n    def debit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Consume turns from the budget. Enforces monotonicity.\"\"\"\n        if turns < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"debit amount must be non-negative\")\n        self.consumed += turns\n\n    def credit(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reimburse turns to the budget.\"\"\"\n        if turns < 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"credit amount must be non-negative\")\n        self.reimbursed += turns\n\n    def can_launch(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for a subprocess launch.\"\"\"\n        return self.available() >= self.minimum_allocation\n\n    def can_remediate(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if enough budget remains for remediation.\"\"\"\n        return self.available() >= self.minimum_remediation_budget\n\n\ndef build_resume_output(\n    config: SprintConfig,\n    halt_task_id: str,\n    remaining_tasks: list[TaskEntry],\n    diagnostic_path: str | None = None,\n    ledger: TurnLedger | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build actionable HALT output with resume command and context.\n\n    Produces an actionable resume block containing:\n    - Resume command with exact task ID\n    - Remaining tasks in execution order\n    - Diagnostic output reference (if available)\n    - Budget suggestion based on remaining work\n\n    Args:\n        config: Sprint configuration.\n        halt_task_id: The first uncompleted task ID.\n        remaining_tasks: Tasks not yet attempted, in execution order.\n        diagnostic_path: Path to diagnostic output (if chain produced results).\n        ledger: TurnLedger for budget suggestion.\n\n    Returns:\n        Formatted HALT output string.\n    \"\"\"\n    remaining_count = len(remaining_tasks)\n    budget_suggestion = max(remaining_count * 10, 50) if remaining_count > 0 else 0\n\n    lines = [\n        \"## HALT — Sprint Paused\",\n        \"\",\n        \"### Resume Command\",\n        f\"```\",\n        f\"superclaude sprint run {config.index_path} --resume {halt_task_id} --budget {budget_suggestion}\",\n        f\"```\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"### Remaining Tasks ({remaining_count})\",\n    ]\n\n    for task in remaining_tasks:\n        lines.append(f\"- {task.task_id}: {task.title}\")\n\n    if diagnostic_path:\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(\"### Diagnostic Output\")\n        lines.append(f\"See: {diagnostic_path}\")\n\n    if ledger:\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(\"### Budget Status\")\n        lines.append(f\"- Consumed: {ledger.consumed} turns\")\n        lines.append(f\"- Available: {ledger.available()} turns\")\n        lines.append(f\"- Suggested budget for resume: {budget_suggestion} turns\")\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ShadowGateMetrics:\n    \"\"\"Aggregated metrics from shadow gate evaluation.\n\n    Collected when ``--shadow-gates`` is enabled. Shadow metrics are\n    informational only and do not affect sprint behavior.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    total_evaluated: int = 0\n    passed: int = 0\n    failed: int = 0\n    latency_ms: list[float] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    @property\n    def pass_rate(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Fraction of gates that passed (0.0–1.0).\"\"\"\n        if self.total_evaluated == 0:\n            return 0.0\n        return self.passed / self.total_evaluated\n\n    @property\n    def p50_latency_ms(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Median gate evaluation latency in milliseconds.\"\"\"\n        if not self.latency_ms:\n            return 0.0\n        s = sorted(self.latency_ms)\n        mid = len(s) // 2\n        if len(s) % 2 == 0:\n            return (s[mid - 1] + s[mid]) / 2\n        return s[mid]\n\n    @property\n    def p95_latency_ms(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"95th percentile gate evaluation latency in milliseconds.\"\"\"\n        if not self.latency_ms:\n            return 0.0\n        s = sorted(self.latency_ms)\n        idx = int(len(s) * 0.95)\n        return s[min(idx, len(s) - 1)]\n\n    def record(self, passed: bool, evaluation_ms: float) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record a single shadow gate result.\"\"\"\n        self.total_evaluated += 1\n        if passed:\n            self.passed += 1\n        else:\n            self.failed += 1\n        self.latency_ms.append(evaluation_ms)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Portify replication takeaways\n\nIf your goal is to replicate the exact patterns for portify code generation, the most important reusable motifs in these files are:\n\n1. **Base/shared models in pipeline**\n   - `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `GateCriteria`, `SemanticCheck`\n   - lightweight, reusable, no sprint imports\n\n2. **Sprint specialization by inheritance**\n   - `SprintConfig(PipelineConfig)`\n   - `SprintStep(Step)`\n   - `PhaseResult(StepResult)`\n   - `ClaudeProcess(_PipelineClaudeProcess)`\n\n3. **Pure-python gate validation**\n   - `gate_passed(...)` is deterministic and tiered\n   - semantic checks are callables on file content\n\n4. **Prompt construction pattern**\n   - `ClaudeProcess.build_prompt()` assembles a structured multiline operational spec\n   - embeds file paths and completion protocol explicitly\n\n5. **TUI rendering pattern**\n   - state object (`MonitorState`) + result object (`SprintResult`) + current execution target (`Phase`)\n   - Rich `Panel` + `Table` + `Progress` composition\n\n6. **Diagnostics pattern**\n   - collect evidence -> classify -> render report\n   - `DiagnosticBundle` is the transport object\n\n7. **Budget/economics pattern**\n   - Each such line indicates\n    72→    one turn was consumed from the budget.\n    73→\n    74→    Args:\n    75→        output_path: Path to the subprocess NDJSON output file.\n    76→\n    77→    Returns:\n    78→        Number of turns counted. Returns 0 if file is missing or empty.\n    79→    \"\"\"\n    80→    try:\n    81→        content = output_path.read_text(errors=\"replace\")\n    82→    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError):\n    83→        return 0\n    84→\n    85→    if not content.strip():\n    86→        return 0\n    87→\n    88→    count = 0\n    89→    for line in content.splitlines():\n    90→        line = line.strip()\n    91→        if line and _TURN_INDICATOR_PATTERN.search(line):\n    92→            count += 1\n    93→\n    94→    return count\n    95→\n    96→\n    97→class OutputMonitor:\n    98→    \"\"\"Background thread that watches a stream-json output file.\n    99→\n   100→    Reads incremental bytes, splits on newlines, and parses each complete\n   101→    line as JSON.  Partial lines are buffered across poll cycles so no\n   102→    data is lost when a write straddles a poll boundary.\n   103→    \"\"\"\n   104→\n   105→    def __init__(self, output_path: Path,     self._seen_files: set[str] = set()\n   114→\n   115→    def start(self):\n   116→        \"\"\"Start the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n   117→        self._stop_event.clear()\n   118→        self._last_read_pos = 0\n   119→        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n   120→        self._seen_files.clear()\n   121→        self.state = MonitorState()\n   122→        self._thread = threading.Thread(\n   123→            target=self._poll_loop,\n   124→            daemon=True,\n   125→            name=\"output-monitor\",\n   126→        )\n   127→        self._thread.start()\n   128→\n   129→    def stop(self):\n   130→        \"\"\"Stop the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n   131→        self._stop_event.set()\n   132→        if self._thread:\n   133→            self._thread.join(timeout=2.0)\n   134→\n   135→    def reset(self, new_output_path: Path):\n   136→        \"\"\"Reset for a new phase (new output file).\"\"\"\n   137→        self.output_path = new_output_path\n   138→        self._last_read_pos = 0\n   139→        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n   140→        self._seen_files.clear()\n   141→        self.state = MonitorState()\n   142→\n   143→    def _poll_loop(self):\n 147→\n   148→    def classify(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> FailureCategory:\n   149→        \"\"\"Determine the most likely failure category.\n   150→\n   151→        Classification priority:\n \"\"\"Collects diagnostic evidence from various sources into a bundle.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig):\n        self.config = config\n\n    def collect(\n        self,\n Reads the debug log (if enabled), output and error files, and\n        captures the monitor state snapshot.\n        \"\"\"\n        bundle = DiagnosticBundle(\n            phase=phase,\n \"watchdog_triggered\" in entry\n                for entry in bundle.debug_log_entries\n            )\n            # Extract last N events\n            bundle.last_events = phase.\"\"\"\n        entries = []\n        in_phase = False\n        try:\n            for line in self.config.debug_log_path.open():\n                line = line.rstrip()\n                if not pass\n        return entries\n\n    def _read_tail(self, path: Path, lines: int = 10) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read the last N lines of a file.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            all_lines = \"\"\"Classifies phase failures based on diagnostic evidence.\"\"\"\n\n    def classify(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> FailureCategory:\n        \"\"\"Determine the most likely failure category.\n\n        Classification priority:\n        1. ERROR: result file indicates error/halt\n        5. Timeout\n        if status == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT or exit_code == 124:\n            evidence.append(f\"Process timeout (exit code {exit_code})\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.TIMEOUT\n\n        # 3. Crash (non-zero exit, not timeout, low stall)\n        if exit_code != 0 and bundle.stall_duration < 30:\n            evidence.append(f\"Process crash (exit code {exit_code}, stall {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s)\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.CRASH\n\n        # 4. Error (result file signals)\n        if status in (PhaseStatus.HALT, PhaseStatus.ERROR):\n            evidence.append(f\"Phase status: {status.value}\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.ERROR\n\n        # 5. Horizontal rules (``---`` with no ``key: value``\n    86→    content) are rejected because the pattern requires at least one\n    87→    ``key: value`` line between delimiters.\n    88→    \"\"\"\n    89→    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n    90→    if match is None:\n    91→        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter not found in {output_file}\"\n    92→\n    93→    frontmatter_text = match.group(1)\n    94→\n    95→    # Extract keys from key: value lines\n    96→    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    97→    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n    98→        line = line.strip()\n    99→        if \":\" in line:\n   100→            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n   101→            if key:\n   102→                found_keys.add(key)\n   103→\n   104→    for field in required_fields:\n   105→        if field not in found_keys:\n   106→            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n   107→\n   108→    return True, None\n   109→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b2ef0efa-f51b-45bb-8b9b-bca7a80178ed","uuid":"8ea0ca34-5462-4457-8609-d7280c31adfc","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/gates.py","content":"\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n\nValidates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n\nNFR-003: No subprocess import. NFR-007: No sprint/roadmap imports.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\ndef gate_passed(output_file: Path, criteria: GateCriteria) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    \"\"\"Validate a step's output against its gate criteria.\n\n    Returns (True, None) on pass.\n    Returns (False, reason) on failure where reason is human-readable.\n    \"\"\"\n    tier = criteria.enforcement_tier\n\n    # EXEMPT: always passes\n    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    if not output_file.exists():\n        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n\n    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n\n    # LIGHT stops here\n    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    lines = content.splitlines()\n    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n        return False, (\n            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n            f\"in {output_file}\"\n        )\n\n    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file)\n        if not ok:\n            return False, reason\n\n    # STANDARD stops here\n    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n        return True, None\n\n    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n            if not check.check_fn(content):\n                return False, f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\"\n\n    return True, None\n\n\n_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(\n    r\"^---[ \\t]*\\n((?:[ \\t]*\\w[\\w\\s]*:.*\\n)+)---[ \\t]*$\",\n    re.MULTILINE,\n)\n\n\ndef _check_frontmatter(\n    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\n\n    Uses regex with re.MULTILINE to discover frontmatter anywhere in the\n    document (not just byte 0), allowing conversational preamble before the\n    ``---`` delimiters.  Horizontal rules (``---`` with no ``key: value``\n    content) are rejected because the pattern requires at least one\n    ``key: value`` line between delimiters.\n    \"\"\"\n    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n    if match is None:\n        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter not found in {output_file}\"\n\n    frontmatter_text = match.group(1)\n\n    # Extract keys from key: value lines\n    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n            if key:\n                found_keys.add(key)\n\n    for field in required_fields:\n        if field not in found_keys:\n            return False, f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\"\n\n    return True, None\n","numLines":109,"startLine":1,"totalLines":109}}}

output_bytes: int = 0\n    output_bytes_prev: int = 0\n    last_growth_time: float = 0.0\n    last_event_time: float = 0.0\n    step_started_at: float = 0.0\n    events_received: int = 0\n    lines_total: int = 0\n    growth_rate_bps: float = 0.0\n    stall_seconds: float = 0.0\n    last_task_id: str = \"\"\n    last_tool_used: str = \"\"\n    files_changed: int = 0\n    findings_detected: int = 0\n\n    @property\n    def stall_status(self) -> str:\n        if self.events_received == 0:\n            elapsed = (\n                time.time() - self.step_started_at if self.step_started_at else 0\n            )\n            return \"waiting...\" if elapsed < 30 else \"STALLED\"\n        if self.stall_seconds > 120:\n            return \"STALLED\"\n        if self.stall_seconds > 30:\n            return \"thinking...\"\n        return \"active\"\n\n    @property\n    def output_size_display(self) -> str:\n        if self.output_bytes < 1024:\n            return f\"{self.output_bytes}B\"\n        return f\"{self.output_bytes / 1024:.1f}KB\"\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

CleanupAuditMonitorState()\n\n    def get_state(self) -> CleanupAuditMonitorState:\n        \"\"\"Return current monitor state.\"\"\"\n        return self.state\n\n    def _poll_loop(self):\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            self._poll_once()\n            self._stop_event.wait(self.poll_interval)\n\n    def _poll_once(self):\n        now = time.monotonic()\n\n        try:\n            size = self.output_path.stat().st_size\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return\n\n        self.state.output_bytes_prev = self.state.output_bytes\n        self.state.output_bytes = size\n\n        if size > self._last_read_pos:\n            chunk = self._read_new_chunk(size)\n            if chunk:\n                self._process_chunk(chunk, now)\n self.state.output_bytes_prev\n        alpha = 0.3\n        self.state.growth_rate_bps = (\n            alpha * (delta / self.poll_interval)\n            + (1 - alpha) * self.state.growth_rate_bps\n        )\n\n    def _read_new_chunk(self, current_size: int) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read only the bytes added since last poll.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(self.output_path, complete NDJSON lines.\"\"\"\n        data = self._line_buffer + chunk\n        lines = data.split(\"\\n\")\n        self._line_buffer = lines[-1]\n\n        for line in lines[:-1]:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n\n            self.state.last_growth_time = now\n            self.state.last_event_time = now\n            self.state.stall_seconds tool = event.get(\"tool\", \"\")\n            if tool:\n                self.state.last_tool_used = tool\n\n        text = json.dumps(event, default=str)\n     )\n\n    def _read_new_chunk(self, current_size: int) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read only the bytes added since last poll.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(self.output_path, errors=\"replace\") as f:\n                f.seek(self._last_read_pos)\n                chunk = f.read(current_size - self._last_read_pos)\n                self._last_read_pos = current_size\n                return chunk\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _process_chunk(self, chunk: str, now: float):\n        \"\"\"Split chunk into lines, parse complete NDJSON lines.\"\"\"\n        data = self._line_buffer + chunk\n        lines = data.split(\"\\n\")\n        self._line_buffer = lines[-1]\n\n        for line in lines[:-1]:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n\n            self.state.last_growth_time = now\n            self.state.last_event_time = now\n            self.state.stall_seconds = 0.0\n            self.state.events_received += 1\n            self.state.lines_total += 1\n\n            try:\n                event = json.loads(line)\n            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n                self._extract_signals_from_text(line)\n                continue\n\n            self._extract_signals_from_event(event)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_event(self, event: dict):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from a parsed NDJSON event.\"\"\"\n        event_type = event.get(\"type\", \"\")\n\n        if event_type == \"tool_use\":\n            tool = event.get(\"tool\", \"\")\n            if tool:\n                self.state.last_tool_used = tool\n\n        text = json.dumps(event, default=str)\n        self._extract_signals_from_text(text)\n\n    def _extract_signals_from_text(self, text: str):\n        \"\"\"Extract task IDs, tool names, file paths from text using regex.\"\"\"\n        task_matches = TASK_ID_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if task_matches:\n            self.state.last_task_id = task_matches[-1]\n\n        tool_matches = TOOL_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if tool_matches:\n            self.state.last_tool_used = tool_matches[-1]\n\n        file_matches = FILES_CHANGED_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        for f in file_matches:\n            self._seen_files.add(f)\n        self.state.files_changed = len(self._seen_files)\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

break\n\n                if time.monotonic() > deadline:\n                    process.stop()\n                    break\n\n                state = monitor.get_state()\n\n                # Stall         ):\n                    if config.stall_action == \"kill\":\n                        process.stop()\n                        break\n\n                try:\n                    tui.update(step, state)\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n\n                time.sleep(0.5)\n\n            # Classify result\n            exit_code = process.wait()\n            monitor.stop()\n            status = _determine_status(exit_code, step, config)\n\n            step_result = CleanupAuditStepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=status,\n CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT,\n            ):\n                collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                bundle = collector.collect(step_result, monitor.state)\n reporter.write(bundle, config)\n\n                result.outcome = CleanupAuditOutcome.HALTED\n                result.halt_step = step.id\n                break\n\n        # Finalize\n        if CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL,\n                    CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT,\n                    CleanupAuditStatus.SKIPPED,\n                )\n                for r in logger.write_summary(result)\n\n    finally:\n        tui.stop()\n        monitor.stop()\n        handler.restore()\n\n    return result\n\n\ndef _build_steps(config: CleanupAuditConfig) -> Gate check for passing steps\n            if status == CleanupAuditStatus.PASS and step.gate:\n                output_file = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n                if output_file.exists():\n                    passed, reason = gate_passed(output_file, step.gate)\n                    if not passed:\n                        step_result.status = CleanupAuditStatus.HALT\n                        step_result.gate_failure_reason = reason\n                        status = CleanupAuditStatus.HALT\n\n            # Handle failures\n            if status not in (\n                CleanupAuditStatus.PASS,\n                CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL,\n                CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT,\n            ):\n                collector = DiagnosticCollector(config)\n                bundle = collector.collect(step_result, monitor.state)\n                classifier = FailureClassifier()\n                category = classifier.classify(bundle)\n                bundle.category = category\n                reporter = ReportGenerator()\n                reporter.write(bundle, config)\n\n                result.outcome = CleanupAuditOutcome.HALTED\n                result.halt_step = step.id\n                break\n\n        # Finalize\n        if result.outcome == CleanupAuditOutcome.SUCCESS:\n            all_passed = all(\n                r.status\n                in (\n                    CleanupAuditStatus.PASS,\n                    CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL,\n                    CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT,\n                    CleanupAuditStatus.SKIPPED,\n                )\n                for r in result.step_results\n            )\n            if not all_passed:\n                result.outcome = CleanupAuditOutcome.ERROR\n\n        result.finished_at = time.time()\n        logger.write_summary(result)\n\n    finally:\n        tui.stop()\n        monitor.stop()\n        handler.restore()\n\n    return result\n\n\ndef _build_steps(config: CleanupAuditConfig) -> list[CleanupAuditStep]:\n    \"\"\"Build the step graph for the cleanup audit pipeline.\"\"\"\n    steps: list[CleanupAuditStep] = []\n\n    # Step G-001: Surface scan\n    steps.append(\n        CleanupAuditStep(\n            id=\"G-001\",\n            prompt=build_surface_scan_prompt(config, []),\n            output_file=Path(\"surface-scan-result.md\"),\n            gate=ALL_GATES[\"G-001\"],\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,\n            pass_type=AuditPassType.SURFACE,\n            agent_type=\"audit-scanner\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    # Step G-002: Structural analysis\n    steps.append(\n        CleanupAuditStep(\n            id=\"G-002\",\n            prompt=build_structural_analysis_prompt(config, [], \"surface-scan-result.md\"),\n            output_file=Path(\"structural-analysis-result.md\"),\n            gate=ALL_GATES[\"G-002\"],\n            timeout_seconds=900,\n            inputs=[Path(\"surface-scan-result.md\")],\n            gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,\n            pass_type=AuditPassType.STRUCTURAL,\n            agent_type=\"audit-analyzer\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    # Step G-003: Per-file profiling (parallel with G-002 output)\n    steps.append(\n        CleanupAuditStep(\n            id=\"G-003\",\n            prompt=build_structural_analysis_prompt(config, [], \"surface-scan-result.md\"),\n            output_file=Path(\"per-file-profiles-result.md\"),\n            gate=ALL_GATES[\"G-003\"],\n            timeout_seconds=900,\n            inputs=[Path(\"surface-scan-result.md\")],\n            gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,\n            pass_type=AuditPassType.STRUCTURAL,\n            agent_type=\"audit-analyzer\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    # Step G-004: Cross-cutting analysis\n    steps.append(\n        CleanupAuditStep(\n            id=\"G-004\",\n            prompt=build_cross_cutting_prompt(config, \"structural-analysis-result.md\"),\n            output_file=Path(\"cross-cutting-result.md\"),\n            gate=ALL_GATES[\"G-004\"],\n            timeout_seconds=900,\n            inputs=[\n                Path(\"structural-analysis-result.md\"),\n                Path(\"per-file-profiles-result.md\"),\n            ],\n            gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,\n            pass_type=AuditPassType.CROSS_CUTTING,\n            agent_type=\"audit-comparator\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    # Step G-005: Consolidation and summary\n    steps.append(\n        CleanupAuditStep(\n            id=\"G-005\",\n            prompt=build_consolidation_prompt(config, \"cross-cutting-result.md\"),\n            output_file=Path(\"consolidation-result.md\"),\n            gate=ALL_GATES[\"G-005\"],\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[Path(\"cross-cutting-result.md\")],\n            gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,\n            pass_type=AuditPassType.CONSOLIDATION,\n            agent_type=\"audit-consolidator\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    # Step G-006: Validation\n    steps.append(\n        CleanupAuditStep(\n            id=\"G-006\",\n            prompt=build_validation_prompt(config, \"consolidation-result.md\"),\n            output_file=Path(\"validation-result.md\"),\n            gate=ALL_GATES[\"G-006\"],\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[Path(\"consolidation-result.md\")],\n            gate_mode=GateMode.BLOCKING,\n            pass_type=AuditPassType.VALIDATION,\n            agent_type=\"audit-validator\",\n        )\n    )\n\n    return steps\n\n\ndef _determine_status(\n    exit_code: int | None,\n    step: CleanupAuditStep,\n    config: CleanupAuditConfig,\n) -> CleanupAuditStatus:\n    \"\"\"Classify step outcome from exit code and output artifacts.\"\"\"\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return CleanupAuditStatus.TIMEOUT\n    if exit_code and exit_code != 0:\n        return CleanupAuditStatus.ERROR\n\n    result_path = config.work_dir / f\"{step.id}-result.md\"\n    output_path = config.work_dir / step.output_file\n\n    if result_path.exists():\n        content = result_path.read_text()\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: HALT\" in content:\n            return CleanupAuditStatus.HALT\n        if \"EXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\" in content:\n            return CleanupAuditStatus.PASS\n        if \"status: PASS\" in content.lower():\n            return CleanupAuditStatus.PASS\n        if \"status: FAIL\" in content.lower():\n            return CleanupAuditStatus.HALT\n        return CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    if output_path.exists():\n        if detect_error_max_turns(output_path):\n            return CleanupAuditStatus.INCOMPLETE\n        return CleanupAuditStatus.PASS_NO_REPORT\n\n    return CleanupAuditStatus.ERROR\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
